# Thread: Two steps forward, one step back
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 50
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-09-15T14:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-51784>

I have been wanting to start a journal for quite some time. Today is the day.

The back story: I have 4 kids (11, 9, 6, 3). The last 3 were born at home, all breastfed until after 2 years old. I have hashimoto's. It started when I was in my early 20s. Synthroid worked "fine" for me, and life kept going. Each pregnancy resulted in a 60 pound weight gain, followed by easy loss over the course of a year - except the last one when the weight loss never happened. I fought off some of the pounds, and was left with an extra 15, but I was weight stable, and could eat "whatever I wanted."

When my 4th was 16 months old, we got a really bad herpes infection. My mouth was covered in painful sores. I could not eat for 10 days, the pain was so bad! I dropped my extra 15 pounds. Then shortly after, I decided to try an elimination diet because my baby seemed to be reacting to all kinds of foods and we couldn't sort it out. Since she was nursing, I did the diet too. Turkey, rice, pears, palm oil, then gradually we added a few things in - butter. Everytime we tried to add in something else, there would be trouble and we'd back off. This went on for 6 weeks. I stayed at my new found low weight. I ate a lot of rice and pears, and brown rice syrup, and rice cakes...

She started receiving immunotherapy shots, and we were gradually able to get off the diet. I started adding in foods like crazy. It was December, and there was lots to add in!! My weight ballooned. 1-3 pounds each day. I gain 15 pounds in 3 weeks, and finally in a panic go to low carb eating. Weight gain stops. But I cannot touch a carb without gaining.

Feb 2011 - I start P90X. I am determined to lose this extra weight. I gain strength and endurance quickly, but no weight loss. I have continual trouble with never feeling full/satiated, ever. I am always thinking about eating, even low carb foods.

Sept 2011 - Continuing the LDA shots with 4th child and myself. The protocol includes 3 days of very limited food choices to minimize cross-reactivity with ingredients in the shots (food/air allergens). I get fed up, and decided I would feel better just fasting. I water fast for 5 days, and I'm delighted to lose the weight! I continue to intermittent fast throughout the fall. I am tired of low carb eating (and starting to gain slowly on the diet), and fasting is the only thing that seems to â€œworkâ€￾ to maintain my weight. I often do days of not eating until 4:30pm onwards. I eat LOTS after 4:30, and I don't restrict. This is my way to â€œhave my cake and eat it too.â€￾ Sometimes I don't eat for 2 days.

Jnauary 2012 - I can't keep up the intermittent fasting to maintain my weight. I gain 8 pounds over 6 days at Christmas, and then I gain another 11 pounds in 3 more weeks eating â€œregularâ€￾. I decide to start looking for other answers.

I do another elimination diet (turkey only) for 2 days, and then slowly add foods back in to figure out which foods are causing such dramatic weight gain/water retention results. I am horrified at the results. Foods that cause quick, dramatic weight gain (1-2 pounds/day):

eggs

all nuts, seeds

dairy

all grains and starches

mushrooms

apples

avocado

green beans

pork, especially cured.

ground chicken, ground pork

suspect foods: summer squash, sauerkruat?

March 2012 - I go to the Women to Women clinic in Maine. They do extensive GI testing, and salivary HPA axis testing. I am insulin resistant. I am at my highest postpartum weight and climbing.

March 2012 - I begin immediately on a strict diet including 3meals/day only (to combat the endless eating habit), and only eat the foods I know are safe (basically meats and vegetables, cocoa is okay too). I start taking insinase, probiotics, and digestive enzymes. I slowly start dropping weight. Aroundt his time I discovered the leptin RX and tweaked it into my plan.

I try to shift from synthroid to Armour thyroid. It is difficult. I end up very hypothyroid on 2 grains, with adrenal symptoms as well (high heart rate, low blood pressure, orthostatic hypo-tension).

June 2012 I begin taking DHEA per saliva labs. I take 4mg/day sublingually. My thyroid dose increases to 4.5 grains of Armour, 25mcg synthroid. I don't seem to absorb much of the T4 in the Armour.

I continue to struggle with high morning heart rates (and low blood pressure), which I attribute to low T4 levels. Labs show reasonable T3, low normal T4. In hindsight, I don't think the low T4 is the cause, but not sure what may be.

July 2012 My weight has really dropped off - 20 pounds since March. I am 3 pounds from my â€œwedding weight,â€￾ previously optimal, never gain or lose weight, but I still cannot deviate on the eating plan without immediate weight gain. I ate a small piece of kielbasa at a BBQ, went up 3 pounds overnight.

August 2012 - I am forced to drop back to synthroid only due to debilitating symptoms. I drop DHEA, Armour, and pregnenelone. High heartrates continue all day long, and low blood pressure. I was no longer functional to care for my family. This takes my T3 from top 1/4 of the range, down to below the bottom end. I feel a different kind of awful! Depression sets in, whereas all summer, even though I felt poorly physically, mentally I was optimistic.

Next entry: the weight I lost while doing the leptin RX, plus adjusting my thyroid meds starts coming back on, 10 pounds over 4 weeks, and I'm losing more foods.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-18T20:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-52466>

Yesterday I finally took the plunge and found fresh raw oysters at a local fish market. The guy shcucked them for me right then and there, put them on ice with lemon, and told me to eat within a hour! No time for procrastination - I went home, and ate my first raw oysters ever. I was expecting to not enjoy them, but I was really very pleasantly surprised! I ate them all, and kept thinking about eating more of them all afternoon.

I seem to be gaining some traction. My energy levels are improving, especially yesterday (was it the oysters, and the iodoral I threw in?). I'm guessing my thyroid hormones are improving. I'll retest in another week or 2 and see where I'm at. Still taking 150mcg tirosint (hoping for better absorption from regular synthroid), and 15mg cytomel. I can feel it when it's time for my next cytomel dose. I start to feel my heart pounding just a little stronger. I feel like there's a little bit or air-hunger. I take the T3 and it all goes away.

I ate an apple yesterday, and expected the worst today. Instead I dropped almost 2 pounds overnight. This in addition to dropping 2 pounds overnight Sunday. Something seems to be working...

I stopped the cycloset 2 days ago, to see if it would help the new neuropathy in my fingers. I am trying to stay calm about it (going to the neurologist on Thursday) until I can get more testing done. Not sure what to test to further rule out (or diagnose) LADA. Trying to stay calm...

Been seeing the sunrise every morning. The past several mornings it comes up a bright orange ball of fireo nthe horizon, just a minute or 2 after I get outside. Spectacular! Blue-blockers are on at dusk, and I'm putting my littlest to bed at 7:30ish (she asks), and I stay in the dark.

I've been avoiding my favorite roasted veggies - brussel sprouts, Kale, and cauliflower. All seem to make me gain weight (water retention). This is new in the past month or so. I'd been eating them all summer long without a problem. I'll try cooking them at lower heat and see how it goes. I dropped down to fish only on Saturday, and was going to slowly add foods back in to see which were causing the water retention (given the 10 pounds increase in the past 4-5 week). I dropped 3.5 pounds overnight. I was pretty surprised considering that's how much I used to dropped after 24 hours of water fasting. I was already low carb going in... Then the next day I failed at my plan of adding the foods in one by one. Ate fish for breakfast, but added in too many things at lunch - some beef in broth (cooked with many vegetables), and then proceeded on to eat 1.5 of the apples we had just picked. Regular epi-paleo dinner. I expected the worst for Sunday morning, but only found I had regained 1 pound.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-19T02:01:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-52567>

Okay something is starting to have some serious traction - 2 days in a row of feeling pretty darn good and energetic. I'd forgotten what it feels like to have energy and actually want to get stuff done! And then, this afternoon, did I finally feel satiety after a meal? Twinges of it anyways. Is it the seafood with an extra push on shellfish lately? The raw oysters yesterday (they're still on my mind)? Is my thyroid hormone dose finally starting to work somewhat reasonably? Have I found the right supplements to support all of this (took a iodoral the past 2 days)? Are the shorter days (less light exposure) helping? I pushed my T3 dose up too. I've been taking 15mg for the past 10ish days and late this afternoon felt the familiar feeling of needing to take my next dose. So I pushed up to 20mg and so far so good... Signing off the computer - it's dark out now!

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-20T15:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-52888>

Never mind the 20mg the other day, yesterday I could barely do 15, and had elevated heart rate for most of the afternoon/evening. I wonder if my T4 meds are actually having an impact and maybe even some converting is going on?

After yesterday's re-read of BG 12, I felt like I had just been landed a terminal illness diagnosis. It was all going well until I read that Hashi's people don't get to use iodine very well because of the destroyed TpO enzyme. I looked at my last labs, and my antibody levels were 82. It's been much higher in the past... I'm not sure if my understanding is correct (and I will have to go look up cites to feel better (worse) about it), because none of the other hashi's people seem to be in a mild panic the way I am. I felt like I should start getting my affairs in order lol!

Off to the neurologist this morning to find out more about the reduced feeling in my fingers, not to mention the elevated heartrates. But after reading B12, I think I get it. Reduced iodine levels make for reduced synaptic function since it's needs in large quantities in the neuron synapses for optimal signalling. The next stop is the epithelial cancers.

### Lyndra — 2012-09-21T00:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-53024>

It sounds like you are doing quite a bit better than a while ago, so that's something to be optimistic about. How say your TPO antibodies have also dropped - how much? are you following a complete AI protocol diet? Good luck with the neurologist, I hope you get the neuropathy figured out.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-21T02:16:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-53053>

The neurologist was uneventful. I passed the tests (I already knew that). He said he wanted to MRI my brain just to make there's no MS starting. I'm not sure how I feel about doing that procedure. The neuropathy has improved (but it's not gone), and I'm wondering if more time with improved circulating thyroid hormones will make it better still. Still wondering how the cycloset fits in (if at all). The neuropathy didn't start until 10 days into taking the cycloset. I've stopped for the time being...

I don't have labs from several years back, but I think my TpO antibodies used to be the 260ish range. Not sure what brought them down specifically, although I wonder if the gluten free diet (3 years) played a big role. I'll ask to get my antibodies tested again in a few more months. Those levels were from April (paleo + AI protocol, but not epi unti mid-summer). Thanks for checking in!

### kathylu — 2012-09-21T02:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-53064>

Just found this listed as a side effect for cycloset:

Musculoskeletal

Musculoskeletal side effects have included muscle cramps, facial pallor, abnormal involuntary movements, ataxia, edema of the feet and ankles, and erythromelalgia.

Rare musculoskeletal side effects including tingling of fingers, cold feet, numbness, muscle cramps in feet and legs, and exacerbation of Raynaud's Syndrome have been reported during bromocriptine therapy.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-21T03:33:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-53080>

Yay that you've had some good days!!!! You are doing so much, you deserve them. I'm sure it's one of those non-linear things, but I bet you get more and more as time goes on.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-25T21:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-54205>

I am feeling much, much better these days than over the summer.

However, I think the sweet spot of my new thyroid regimen went sailing by last week, and now I'm crossing into the land of over-driven. I can't seem to handle any T3 anymore. My heartrate is high into the afternoons now (80 just now), but blood pressure is holding reasonable. I just feel over-pushed. I had been doing the equivalent of 150mcg T4/day, but I've dropped down to 112, and had blood drawn this morning. My endo won't run an RT3. She says the Mayo Clinic found the lab to be non-reproduceable... I haven't checked into it yet to refute it, but she did agree to run an HS-CRP instead. I am also supping 10000iu of D/day now, and my level a month ago was 54 (I went UP over the summer, 10 points, without supplementing!! Maybe I'm not so horrendously broken after all??!). I'm wondering if this is somehow pushing me more hyper. Gotta say I'm feeling pretty darn great though the past week or more.

Tomorrow morning I will have the MRI of my brain... I couldn't decide whether to go through with it or not, but at this point I've run out of cancellation time.

Still have lots of food issues. Went apple picking with the kids on Saturday, and ate an apple. Then on Sunday I ate a small amount of cantaloupe I was cutting up for them for their lunches. Yesterday I was up 2.5 pounds in the morning, complete with my usual sore left wrist that goes along. I dialed back in my diet and this morning I dropped 3 pounds... I'm taking this as a good sign. In the past it would take several days to clear that kind of inflammation. Now it is leaving more quickly.

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-26T20:32:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-54492>

Did the MRI this morning. I didn't think I was claustrophobic... until I saw where they planned to put me!! The procedure went fine. I settled in after about 5 mintues and felt calm enough to lie with my eyes opened. My procedure involved an IV contrast as well, for extra creepiness. Overall, it's the kind of procedure that leaves you feeling somewhat "handled," for lack of better words, despite wonderful staff, etc. I wasn't sure whether or not to go through with the scan, but it's done now.

Did my thyroid labs yesterday, and I am trying to be patient for the results. I had pushed my T3 all the way to 15mg, but have gradually found I needed to back to down. Today I've done 7.5 so far. Not sure if I will add in another 2.5 later in the day or not. Yesterday I took no T3 (I usually take 5 first thing in the morning, and I didn't want it to be in my bloodwork, and then I didn't feel like I needed it later). And put on of the pounds I had just finished peeing out the day before. And my diet was rock solid. Sooo, T3 levels play a huge role in my level of water retention. I'll have to read up on this some more. And today I feel a little more hypo than my new usual.

Dh has been out of town the past few nights, and usually means crappier sleep for me. All the sounds of the night are suddenly only my responsibility, and I'm sole protector of my sleeping children! I'm ready, I'm oxidizing all night long lol!!

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-28T20:46:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-55052>

Wow - oysters roasted (400, 20 minutes!) with green onions and garlic on top - wow!! I haven't been enjoying this necessary organism very much, and have been avoiding them. Now I need to figure out how to source more!!

I seem to be still trending upwards, feeling better and better. Brain fog is lifting, energy level is improving, overall I am feeling more stable. Still awaiting thyroid bloodwrok results from Tuesday. Could it be that T4 therapy with a touch of T3, is actually being effective?

I have been taking magnesium today (citrate), trying to find bowel tolerance (and beyond). I've been using the powdered calm form dissolved in hot water, and I've been drinking and drinking, and still not getting results... Yesterday I took magnesium malate, tons of it, and not results. How much do I need to take...?

My weight and water fluctuations are still terrible.

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-28T21:28:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-55058>

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-28T22:20:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-55076>

It wasn't the first few days... I just took the full dose of Calm twice and then I was off to the races for four days. I'm sure it's all an individual thing. What was odd to me was that even after I stopped taking the mg. I had the runs for quite a while after. ### bigknitwit — 2012-10-01T19:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-55603>

Mg doesn't seem to be giving me the consistent results that I think are needed for this biofilm business. I've been reading up about them, and it seems there's a lot to breaking them up, not to mention the systemic effects that are felt as they are compromised and bacteria is released. This site seems to have a lot of thought provoking information. [http://coolinginflammation.blogspot.com/2009/09/cure-for-inflammatory-diseases.html](http://http://coolinginflammation.blogspot.com/2009/09/cure-for-inflammatory-diseases.html) Also other places I've looked at mention that this process can take many months - the right enzymes taken between meals can perforate these biofilms and start to compromise them... I still need to hear the Q&A from the webinar (will check next if it's up yet) to hopefully grab more info.

Meanwhile, on the homefront, I'm feeling very proud of myself. Last night we had a pipe start leaking raw sewage (which was thankfully shower water at that time) into the basement. What a mess! Dh and I were up for many hours dealing with the aftermath of all that water. Me up past 9pm is an extra-ordinary day lol, and there we were with towels and buckets and bleach. I didn't have my blueblockers on, and I was very thankful for all that artificial light lol! We contained the problem, but dh couldn't figure out if it was a partial plug in one of our pipes, or into the septic tank. He went out to the septic tank and didn't notice anything. We still had minimal water use. This morning the plumber was supposed to come... Well, I decided to get out open the septic tank, and take another look, and what should I see but a gigantic wad of toilet paper plugging the incoming pipe. I grab a stick, clear the plug, and the water starts flowing again. Hooray!! I cancel the plumber. All my children are going out there this afternoon after school to see the final destination of all things put down the toilet. Maybe that will help curb excessive toilet paper use... Dh can stick to making microchips, I'll handle the home-front

Now back to all things epi-paleo...

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-16T01:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-58453>

Posting otday because it's been some time...

I'm 13 pounds up since I hit my "goal" weight back in August. I am trying not to worry about it, but it's frustrating. I am hoping that as my thyroid labs improve, the weight will slow, stop, and then reverse..? Here's hoping... Meanwhile it's showing me how much my weight responds to T3 blood levels. I never used to have a weight issue, even when very hypoT. In the past when my meds needed adjusting, my (post-partum, effortless) weight loss would just cease until the issue was corrected (with more T4), and then I'd continue downward. Now it's different, not to mention I'm not absorbing my T4 much. That never used to happen either.

I started taking HCL with my meals. I figure that if I'm hypoT, my HCL levels are probably lower than they should be. What I notice of significant difference is that I feel more HUNGRY, especially an hour or 2 after a meal. My stomach feels different... The hunger feels almost like the endless hunger I used to feel in the first few months of pregnancy (without the nausea). No more undigested food passing through either.

I am still struggling with never feeling satisfied after eating a meal. I always wish I could keep on eating. Maybe I am getting a little traction - when i eat my BAB, I don't feel like I want to eat more of it. In the summer, I did, even at crazy high amounts of protein. I guess that's something. But it's not turning off the desire for "other" foods, that's for sure. That is an endless battle.

Honestly, I am struggling to keep my resolve and stay with the program. I am eating more apples that I should be, and I know there's no wiggle room left for me given the maturity of my Hashi's. I feel like once I get my thyroid hormones at a reasonable level, it will be worth a shot again. Right now feels like endless uphill climbing and I'm starting to wear down...

What I notice of significant difference is that I feel more HUNGRY, especially an hour or 2 after a meal. My stomach feels different... The hunger feels almost like the endless hunger I used to feel in the first few months of pregnancy (without the nausea). No more undigested food passing through either.
>

Maybe I am getting a little traction - when i eat my BAB, I don't feel like I want to eat more of it. In the summer, I did, even at crazy high amounts of protein. I guess that's something. But it's not turning off the desire for "other" foods, that's for sure. That is an endless battle.
>

> Honestly, I am struggling to keep my resolve and stay with the program. I am eating more apples that I should be, and I know there's no wiggle room left for me given the maturity of my Hashi's. I feel like once I get my thyroid hormones at a reasonable level, it will be worth a shot again. Right now feels like endless uphill climbing and I'm starting to wear down...

Mornin' BKW. I am in your shoes and never had a weight issue either even when extremely E dominant. Weight gain isn't fun and now my pants are getting too tight too wear. I think it is a thyroid issue that needs to be resolved but I really don't know what is going on. My dh said I it might be that I am actually getting something out of my food now. It sounds like for you the weight gain is the last 2 months. Sort of seems like "fattening" up for the long, cold, dark winter.

This is all tough to take and do and seems quite unfair at times. But, I have to ask you, if you go "off" the protocol, where do you go from here? How bad will things be and what kind of diet trouble will you be in? Apples may not be the best but they aren't apple pie either. I made a decision a few weeks ago after talking with my doc that I was going to eat a few veggies as I had cut most of them out. Not optimal in Jack's eyes but this is my life and I need to live it within my boundaries and it is my "report" card. This has certainly helped me stay the course. I still eat a BAB with a little squash. Lunch is seafood with a little sweet potato or something with a mufa fat if any. So still trying to follow the rules. Pretty soon I will cut back on those and get to a much lower carb point but for the moment it is what I needed to do. Perhaps you should cut yourself some slack until you get past this feeling and then step your game up. A lot of people would disagree with me but I know how hard I struggle and it seems you are on the same page as I was recently. Kinda funny, right now the fat in my meat is really tasting good to me. I used to despise fat. Like you said, you might just need to get your thyroid hormones in balance (I know, easier said than done) and the ship will start righting itself. We are also women and go thru hell every month with our thoughts.

Did the hunger issue get better or worse with the HCL? Are you CT'ing? That drove my hunger up a lot. It dropped but never like some people's out here. Hell, I have been doing BAB's for 1.5 years and I still am hungry at noon for lunch. We are just a little more broken and need more time and fixing. Have you done the Metametics GI Effects test to see what is really going on in your gut? Lot's to be told by that test.

All of this is really hard for me as I used to be a really good cook and enjoyed serving a really nice meal. Now I heat up the same thing a couple times a day some days, fill my gut and call it good. Most days, I am also too tired to cook by the time I get home anyway.

I think you have just hit a bump in the road and it needs a little time to settle. I hope things turn soon for you. No fun to be feeling this way!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-10-16T16:23:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-58518>

Please hang in there BKW! I know how frustrating it is to gain weight (I gained 15 lbs on the reset in a few months), but look at the big picture: are you feeling better? Do you have more energy? Do you sleep better? Is your mood better? It might not be linear, but if you see overall improvment, it means you are getting there. As soon as your labs will get better, the weight will come off. It's telling you sometimes is wrong and that you have to work on it.

I also know what you mean by feeling always hungry. That should stabilize everytime soon. If you are truly hungry, just eat!! See if you lower your carbs if it's better. And what if you drop HCL? I was hypo and never took any. Might not the the best, but you can try it. In CT7, Dr K talks about ghrelin and how it stimulates appetite...might be worth a read. When we understand, we do better

Have a nice day, and come back here if you need support!! K

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-16T16:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-58519>

> **vkiernan;60020 wrote:** Mornin' BKW. I am in your shoes and never had a weight issue either even when extremely E dominant. Weight gain isn't fun and now my pants are getting too tight too wear. I think it is a thyroid issue that needs to be resolved but I really don't know what is going on. My dh said I it might be that I am actually getting something out of my food now. It sounds like for you the weight gain is the last 2 months. Sort of seems like "fattening" up for the long, cold, dark winter.

In my case, I was taking the most T3 back in August (36mg) via Armour. I had gradually increased to that level over the course of the summer. Then, I abruptly stopped all T3 meds, and moved to T4 only, because my heartrate was so high, and my blood pressure was so low, and I couldn't function, except in the cold tub! Dropping to T4 only, and then a month later adding in 10mg T3 (now I'm taking 15), I think, was what got the weight gain going. Then about 2 or 3 weeks ago, I started reading about stripping biofilms using Mg, and I thought I'd give it a try (originally I thought it would only take a few days. Now I know it's a much more involved process). Somehow, I decided, that it would be a good time to eat some nuts (I struggle with wanting to eat them every single day). So I did, and then I did the next day, and the day after. And then I would stop the nuts for a few days (swearing them off), only to restart again. My weight has been creeping up faster, and it's almost always in 1 pound increments overnight (I gained a pound between yesterday and today, and same with the day before that). My eyes are swollen, my fat jeans are tight. And I feel like a bit of a failure. I've been pristine with my AI diet until I let my guard down and started on the nuts. Well, I'm running out of clothes to wear, so today is the day to start fresh. Fish for breakfast, fish for lunch if I want it, and then maybe a steak for dinner. No carbs, no snacks, back on the protocol like I used to be. I did a fish only day a month or so ago and dropped 3.5 pounds overnight. That's what I used to drop after a 24 hour fast... Much easier than water fasting!! I'm trying to say to myself, "I _don't eat that," as opposed to, "I won't_ eat that." I often sautee green onions, ginger, and coleslaw (raw, pre-chopped, from the bag!) with my fish (with scallops is great good). I will move back to that routine, if I can stand a few days of fish/meat only.

This is all tough to take and do and seems quite unfair at times. But, I have to ask you, if you go "off" the protocol, where do you go from here? How bad will things be and what kind of diet trouble will you be in? Apples may not be the best but they aren't apple pie either. I made a decision a few weeks ago after talking with my doc that I was going to eat a few veggies as I had cut most of them out. Not optimal in Jack's eyes but this is my life and I need to live it within my boundaries and it is my "report" card. This has certainly helped me stay the course. I still eat a BAB with a little squash. Lunch is seafood with a little sweet potato or something with a mufa fat if any. So still trying to follow the rules. Pretty soon I will cut back on those and get to a much lower carb point but for the moment it is what I needed to do. Perhaps you should cut yourself some slack until you get past this feeling and then step your game up. A lot of people would disagree with me but I know how hard I struggle and it seems you are on the same page as I was recently. Kinda funny, right now the fat in my meat is really tasting good to me. I used to despise fat. Like you said, you might just need to get your thyroid hormones in balance (I know, easier said than done) and the ship will start righting itself. We are also women and go thru hell every month with our thoughts.

I had been cutting myself a bunch of slack (apples, nuts, other fruits too. Still paleo, but not nearly as LC, and not AI friendly from those blasted NUTS!), and now I'm wearing it... So, I know the process is self limiting, unless I like buying a new size of clothes every week. The thing is, everyone wants me to take "a day off" for Thanksgiving. "Surely, you'll take a day off your diet for Thanksgiving, won't you?" I wish it were that easy.. I think, in a pinch I will allow myself more fruits/veggies, but I really need to stay on the AI protocol, difficult and limiting as it is. For me, one day off suddenly leads to a landslide of over-indulgence, in anticipation of the upcoming "clamp-down!" It's really awful. I never used to have to think about food choices, or portions. I just ate, and wondered what the big deal was... I will keep the carbs (veggies, apples if really desperate) lowish, but not winter JK style, until I get my thyroid supplementation in a better place. I have no operating gland left, so when I don't get enough from the pills, there's literally nothing more. And I find when I'm really hypoT, I can' tolerate the really low carb diet as well.

> Did the hunger issue get better or worse with the HCL? Are you CT'ing? That drove my hunger up a lot. It dropped but never like some people's out here. Hell, I have been doing BAB's for 1.5 years and I still am hungry at noon for lunch. We are just a little more broken and need more time and fixing. Have you done the Metametics GI Effects test to see what is really going on in your gut? Lot's to be told by that test.
>
> !
>
>
> My CTing has also dropped away, with going really hypoT over the past few months. Things are stabilizing a little now, i should get started again. I keep the house cold, and try to stay under-dressed at least for the mornings. I did the Genova Diagnostics GI test back in the spring before starting any diet. NO yeast, no parasites, low bifido bacteria, slightly butyrate levels, a few other things. Nothing really glaring came from it (at least according to the practitioner who ordered it for me).
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> All of this is really hard for me as I used to be a really good cook and enjoyed serving a really nice meal. Now I heat up the same thing a couple times a day some days, fill my gut and call it good. Most days, I am also too tired to cook by the time I get home anyway.

I feel like I've entered Oz or something. Food used to be fun and good, and whatever I wanted and I felt fine and great. Now, I have to cook all kinds of wonderful foods for my family, and not touch them myself. I think that is the worst of it. Feeling left out, and getting stuck with all the work!! Tomorrow is Dh's birthday, and I still don't know what my plan is for that. I need to prepare him something "special." But it annoys him that I won't eat the food. Heh - in the summer, at my lowest weight, he was worried I was becoming anorexic and wanted me to track my calories. I did, and showed him that I eat roughly 2200 calories/day. Now that the weight is going on and I'm complaining about weight gain, he's saying it's probably because of all those calories I'm eating!! I'm eating exactly the same way (except for the nuts the past few weeks)!! Good Grief!! He is a die-hard calories-in, calories-out person.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-16T20:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-58574>

All the swelling leads me to thyroid issues, especially the eyes. Mine have been swollen since I have been on t3 only. I want to do the t3 only protocol though to clear my rt3 so I am living with it for now. Are you able to drop some of the t4 and add a little more t3? You made some very abrupt changes to your system and it might just be in shock a bit. You don't have your thyroid anymore? Wouldn't you have been better to cut back on the Armour instead of stopping all the t3? Just thinking out loud. I don't have a ton of experience in this area.

It sounds like the nuts are an issue. Maybe swap the fruit/veggies for the nuts and see how you fair but still keep them limited. Good luck with the fish days and so glad you are not giving up.

As for Thanksgiving, people don't and won't understand that you cannot eat those things if you wish to remain healthy. They clearly think it is about weight loss and nothing to do with being healthy. I'm still preaching to my dh. As much as he has heard from me, he still doesn't get it. I made him fried fish (flour coated) for dinner last night and he kept offering me a bite. Kept telling him I can't. Hold to your guns and do what's right for you. Remind them that eating once will cause a flareup for up to 6 months.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-16T21:40:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-58578>

I think the swelling is also in part due to bad thyroid levels. I do have a gland, but I need to take full replacement doses (and then some), and basically get nothing out of what's left of it. I get such little absorption of T4 thatit's already pretty low. Doc won't test my RT3 (says test is non-reproduceable). Honestly, I wonder about the T3 protocol. If you find the underlying reason for your high RT3, then in theory, shouldn't the problem start to correct itself as you address that reason? Otherwise, I see the T3 only protocol as "forcing" the issue, without addressing the cause.

The problem for me, on Armour, was that at 2 grains, I was very hypoT, at 3 grains, I was all over the place, and as I increased the dose, things got worse, not better. I wasn't absorbing the T4 almost at all. At my worst, I was on 50mcg synthroid, plus 4.5 grains of Armour, and my T4 levels had just crossed into the lower end of the normal range. My doctor and I, at the time, decided that after 4 months of fiddling to get it right and only making things worse, it was time to "reset." Drop all T3 and start over again. Meanwhile, I also switched doctors. I had been driving 2.5 hours each way, and it was becoming unsustainable, especially given my need for frequent lab work. So now I am seeing someone in town. At least she is open to me increasing the T3 a bit (from 10 to 15). I am hoping to encourage her to allow me to move to 20 at some point, and maybe even 25. We'll see.

If I tell my dh eating these items once can cause a prolonged response, he will not believe it, and ask for more data. I can't keep all the data in my mind, and I come up short. I try not to say much at all, because I don't have enough background to really know what I'm talking about, and because it's straining for him whenever it comes up.

> **vkiernan;60104 wrote:** All the swelling leads me to thyroid issues, especially the eyes. Mine have been swollen since I have been on t3 only. I want to do the t3 only protocol though to clear my rt3 so I am living with it for now. Are you able to drop some of the t4 and add a little more t3? > It sounds like the nuts are an issue. Maybe swap the fruit/veggies for the nuts and see how you fair but still keep them limited. Good luck with the fish days and so glad you are not giving up.

They clearly think it is about weight loss and nothing to do with being healthy. I'm still preaching to my dh. As much as he has heard from me, he still doesn't get it. I made him fried fish (flour coated) for dinner last night and he kept offering me a bite. Kept telling him I can't. Hold to your guns and do what's right for you. Remind them that eating once will cause a flareup for up to 6 months.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-16T23:49:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-58600>

BKW, I'm not sure this will be helpful, but when I start to get mired down in the details of my health, I try to regain the 30k foot view. I think we need both. This is a parallel with the Quilt as the founding document and the particulars of our n=1. What I hear is that you are focussed in on T3/thyroid and see that as something that needs fixing before other things can fall into place. What I think I've learned from the Quilt is that it's all interconnected. You can't heal in a linear fashion. You can't wait for one thing to get better before fixing something else. You heal things simultaneously AND at different rates. The labs and specifics help quantify your n =1 and help tweak the direction you need to move in. But the overall picture is still your general guidance. In simple terms, live as you believe a healthy person lives. Don't wait until you're healthy to live that way... that ensures it can't come to pass.

The other thing I think of when I read your entries is the background 'elephant in the living room' (or, in this case, in the bedroom). Your husband's lack of support is difficult for me to read about. Which I would then guess means it is very difficult to live with. It seems that his lack of support and questioning of your methods pushes you to believe you need to get 'smarter' and be able to counteract his every doubt with Dr Kruse-level reasoning. While I hope someday to understand this inside and out like Dr Kruse does, that day (at least for me) is a long way off. But I think you can do something counterintuitive here... instead of trying to answer him with scientific brilliance (and instead of shutting down/shutting up because you feel you can't do that), see if you can access a part of you that is more heart-centered or intuitive. I'm thinking of something like "this feels very right to me and it's pretty clear that trying to do what 'logic' and conventional medicine has dictated is very wrong. I may not be able to explain it, but I understand it. I need you to support me. Or at the very least, get out of my way so I can support myself." You don't have to have all the answers. And you have no one to answer to except yourself.

I don't mean to make it sound easy. I have just found that sometimes I need to do the opposite to what I'm currently doing or to try something I've never done in order to break open new ways of being.

Last thought re: your constant desire to eat, no matter how full. I think this is where the idea of thoughts creating reality can come in. My guess is the tie-in here is the theme of deprivation. You are physiologically full, but you 'feel' deprived. Hence you feel the need to eat more (and likely to eat the things you are 'deprived' of). Instead there are other ways to feel about food... food as 'hormone juice'... food as 'Ferrari fuel' (not as entertainment), to quote two of Dr Kruse's metaphors. I also like to think about food as self-care. Wim Hof walks around saying 'the cold is my friend.' I use a version of that with my food and it helps cut through the extraneous thinking that can interfere with optimal diet and living.

Hang in there. (But don't forget to let go ).

It really resonates, and makes me really think about how I've gotten where I'm at, and how I should proceed to improve my family relationships. I really appreciate it, and yes, it was very, very helpful - thank you again for "sticking your neck out."

I agree. It gave me a lot to think about too and was very helpful. I was thinking this morning about you Lauren and wondered if the epi-p diet was making you sooo insightful or the experiences you've faced. I love reading whatever you write. You are gentle, kind and compassionate.

### bigknitwit — 2012-11-02T19:20:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-61576>

Updates updates

1\. I finally figured out why my abdomen (belly button) is sensitive. I have an umbilical hernia. I've probably had it a while, but now recently it has started bothering me enough for me to notice, figure out what it was and go to the doctor. I will have surgery for it in a week! Time to start icing. Doctor wants to put in a mesh-like piece of plastic that covers the area, and stays in. He says they used to just stitch the muscles back together, but the mesh makes for less pain and a faster recovery. I'm not excited about the mesh, or anything foreign left inside me. I am hoping he will consider doing the procedure without the mesh. I'll take the pain and suffering!!

2\. I have really tightened my diet up over the past several days (due to bloating, and lots of discomfort on the hernia). I had been allowing myself a little more in-season carbs than usual - apples, pears, carrots, squash. I was gaining, my eyes were swelling, and my wrist was hurting, so, it became clear once again that I couldn't continue (despite trying with digestive enzymes). Now, weight is pouring out of me! I've dropped 5 pounds in 3 days, eating fish, chocolate, and a small amount of beef, as well as a baby carrot or two. I have done this before and found similar results. However, this time, I don't feel as deprived, almost not at all really. I hope this feeling stays!

Since starting this whole healing process, I have been telling myself I am trying to heal my body, but there's this other, more sinister view that sits on my shoulder, nagging me. It hopes I can heal myself enough so that I can go back to enjoying some of the foods, just like I used to, without obvious consequence (never mind the autoimmune disease, the insomnia, etc. This devilish view does not come from a place of higher thinking). When I started all this early in the year, I cleared out all the hidden crap I had around the house - my own stashes. It filled a large rubbermaid container! Ridiculous. I put this container in the pool house, and left it there. In those early months, I thought about that container multiple times a day. How much I would love to get back into it - the satisfaction, etc. I ate lindt 90% instead (lots). I eventually purged the junk exactly where it belonged (the dump), but I couldn't fathom that for many months. It's almost like I needed to think that the opportunity was there. Now, I am finding myself less and less tempted. I am not spending so much time thinking about the foods I can't have. I feel as if that sinister piece is starting to lift. There is life after junk food addiction, and I'm starting to glimpse it!

### Endless — 2012-11-04T22:50:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-62088>

Wow, awesome insight! I am grateful for these constant reminders I get by reading about other people's issues It's SO EASY to let carbs creep up, especially when they seem so healthy and benign. I'm experiencing the same thing with apples and squash. Thanks for the lightbulb moment!

### bigknitwit — 2012-11-05T22:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-62256>

Wow - I'm glad I wrote something that might actually help somebody else! I'm starting to get it drilled into my thick skull that I just shouldn't even try these kinds of foods for a good long while. If it was a fail this week, then it will also fail next week. I need to push through the winter in "winter mode." I'm also starting to feel so much less deprived, and much more at peace with it.

> **Endless;63865 wrote:** Wow, awesome insight! I am grateful for these constant reminders I get by reading about other people's issues It's SO EASY to let carbs creep up, especially when they seem so healthy and benign. I'm experiencing the same thing with apples and squash. Thanks for the lightbulb moment!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-10T18:30:14+0200

mmmmm liver in any way shape or form YUMMMMM

### bigknitwit — 2012-11-12T23:20:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-63874>

The evening of the canned oysters actually ended with me feeling pretty nauseous 6 hours after eating them. Of course who knows if it was the oysters or not, but I'm not eating them canned again! No problem with fresh in this regard.

I had my umbilical hernia surgery this morning. I dutifully iced my stomach an hour or more each day for the past 10 days or so. I have ice on my stomach again right now. The vicodin is wearing off and the most painful thing for me right now is my throat (thanks to the breathing apparatus they put in there). I am hoping to not need any more pain meds, but we will see as the evening progresses. At my request, the surgeon did not use any mesh, just sutures that will eventually disolve (many months, he said). I'd rather that than something foreign left in me. So, overall, I'm feeling pretty good, and I'm glad it's behind me.

I just got labs for DD (almost 4), and it looks like her thyroid levels are being over-replaced.

TSH less than 0.01

FT3 7.8 3.4-5.1 (fasting, am, before any Armour)

FT4 1.8 0.9- 1.6

Doctors also ran her lipids, which I didn't write down. He said they were all good (I asked for copies of her labs). Vitamin D was 44, SElenium was 116 (40-103), and her glucose was 77. Dropping her Armour from 80mg/day to 60, retest in 6 weeks (oh how fun with a preschooler...)

### Thor — 2012-11-13T02:43:02+0200

like doing the two step

### vkiernan — 2012-11-13T02:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-63919>

Hardest part was staying away from doing laundry (cuz somebody has to do it) and picking up little ones (somebody has to do that too). Somebody IS helping you with the kiddos - yes??? And the laundry???? I hope you feel better very soon.

### bigknitwit — 2012-11-13T15:44:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-64015>

Well, I definitely needed the pain meds all last night. the pain started to really kick in during the night. This morning though I am managing with Ibuprofen. All my kids are at school until 3pm, when I need to drive one to gymanstics practise (so no more narcotics for me). I got all the laundry caught up on the weekend. For the next little while, DH will carry the basket down in the morning, I will do the wash, and he will bring it back up in the evening (no lifting for me). Even though I ate very little yesterday, I still put on 2 pounds... I wonder if that's normal after surgery, or if this is my regular inflammatory reaction to one of more of the meds or anesthesia. My eyes are all swollen (just like when I eat a reactive food). I avoided all eating while there, and just siped some water before being discharged. They offered me crackers.... My life seems soo far removed from cracker eating. I forget that would be considered a "normal" snack.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-13T20:35:50+0200

Drink a lot of water and flush the anesthesia out of you. Nice to hear hubby is helping at least in the hubby kind of way. Hope you have a better night tonight.

### bigknitwit — 2012-11-13T21:33:14+0200

I wonder if I should take more pregnenelone, or just take a few of those hydrocortisone tablets I have left lying around from way back when... I think I'll just deal with today and see how tomorrow feels. I'm squeamish about taking the bandage off tomorrow!

### bigknitwit — 2012-12-10T09:57:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-steps-forward-one-step-back.3676/post-67001>

After a bunch of weeks away (since Thanksgiving?), I'm trying to get acquainted with this new board and back to doing some posting.

Things have started to improve for me. I am now taking 20mg T3 (increased from 15), as well as 150mcg T4. I do 10 first thing in the morning, then 7.5 at 1ish, and 2.5 at 5pm. I think I still need more T3, but I'm going to have a hard time convincing my doctor. I think I need a bit more for my morning dose, and then 10 at the 1pm dose, and probably 5 at 5pm. I have noticed since increasing to 20 that my high heartrate going to bed has really dropped off. We'll see where I'm at after a few weeks on this dose. I find it takes that long to get the full impact of the increased T3.Â  I still have a jumpy heartrate in the morning though, especially after breakfast. My blood sugar control is much better now than it was in the summer on Armour (more T3 overall). I'm starting to wonder if I had such a terrible time with Armour because it is pig, and I can't seem to eat pork without a light reaction.

Â 

Meanwhile, I have also been eating more carbs... It has made a HUGE difference in my energy levels. With better blood sugar, I found I am able to eat sweet potato without a reaction, and a few fruits (grapes, apples, cranberries). I feel like I am cheating, because with the low light levels I should be in full ketosis. But the combination of the carbs and the increased T3 has produced such a positive result. My house is getting cleaned, prepped for Christmas. I've been out shopping, walking, etc. I am starting to feel energetic, instead of just struggling to throw together dinner. And I'm sleeping better. I've been sleeping through the night more often than not - unheard for me. I'm wondering if the carbs also helped increase my T3 levels. I'm not sure.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-09-15T18:02:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ot-world-rivers-day.3681/post-51819>

**World Rivers Day is on September 29, 2012. Some of my friends from **350.org** and the Thompson Environmental Network** want to meet down at the Thompson River for a garbage clean-up day. I really wanted to go pick plastic bags and garbage out of the river but I have to work on Saturdays. I still had the urge to help. It got me thinking about how the garbage gets into the river in the first place.

I realized I didn’t need to go anywhere...

[http://eatkamloops.org/solidarity-of-a-sort/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/solidarity-of-a-sort/)

I invite you to clean the rivers of the world with me without leaving your neighborhood. If you would like, please post pictures of your neighborhood garbage. I am wondering if the garbage will look the same everywhere or if it is different.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-09-16T20:31:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ot-world-rivers-day.3681/post-51955>

# Thread: typer two diabetes is cell dedifferentiation?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/typer-two-diabetes-is-cell-dedifferentiation.3682/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### chocolate — 2012-09-15T18:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/typer-two-diabetes-is-cell-dedifferentiation.3682/post-51820>

[http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/notrocketscience/2012/09/14/diabetes-bourne-identit/](http://http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/notrocketscience/2012/09/14/diabetes-bourne-identit/)

> And like Jason Bourne, these pre-beta cells can take on new fatesâ€”they can turn into other pancreatic cells, which make other hormones. These include glucagon, which acts as insulinâ€™s antagonist, opposing its effects on blood sugar levels. This explains why a diabetic pancreas not only loses the ability to make insulin, but starts making more glucagon instead.
>
>
>
> Acciliâ€™s team member Chutima Talchai found that the beta-cells depend on a protein called FoxO1 to stop themselves from dedifferentiating. Without this handler, they spontaneously reverted to the pre-beta state whenever the mice experienced bouts of physical stress, such as pregnancy or even ageing.
>
>
>
> In humans, Accili thinks that a continuous storm of fats and carbohydrates could have the same effect. His working hypothesis is that a bad diet stresses the beta-cells, forcing them to produce insulin beyond their normal capacity. Rather than work themselves to death, the cells enact a self-preservation programme that turns them back into a simpler state that isnâ€™t responsible for making insulin. (At this point, it becomes impossible to stretch the Bourne metaphor without spoilers. Go see the film. Thereâ€™s a nice bit with a magazine.)
>
>
>
> Itâ€™s an intriguing idea, and one that he will try to confirm in a later study. Also to be confirmed: whether this applies to humans as well as mice. **Peter Butler, a diabetes specialist at the University of California, Los Angeles, praised Acciliâ€™s study but told me that many discoveries in diabetic mice have failed to translate to humans.**
>
>
>
> This is clearly the big question to answer: does the pancreas of someone with type 2 diabetes have more of these pre-beta cells than a healthy person? If so, it may be possible to devise new treatments for the condition by finding compounds that can stop the beta-cells from dedifferentiating.

I'm going with this thinking because of the German study on stress and the brain cells reproducing without oxytocin receptors or whatever version, rather than get worked to death.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-15T21:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/typer-two-diabetes-is-cell-dedifferentiation.3682/post-51850>

mice and rats never equal humans......... Only obesity researchers think they do.

# Thread: Hack my Labs - Input Please!

**Posts:** 7

### angieonthegogo — 2012-09-15T21:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-labs-input-please.3683/post-51851>

TSH 2.220 uIU/mL 0.450-4.500

T4 9.4 ug/dL 4.5-12.0

FT3 2.8 pg/mL 2.0-4.4

FT4 1.25 ng/dL 0.82-1.77

rT3 42.1 ng/dL 13.5-34.2 HIGH

TPO Ab 11 IU/mL 0-34

Antithy Ab 39

LDL 103 mg/dL 0-99 HIGH

Free Testosterone 0.4 pg/mL 0.0-2.2

Progesterone 4.4 ng/mL

Estradiol 98.7 pg/mL

All labs done on approx CD 22 of 30. 33 yo F. In looking at these I obviously have a T4-T3 conversion problem right? rT3 is high, LDL is high but believe it or not it has come down from 128 last Oct. HDL is still low but up from 38. My TC is down from 193 and Trigs are way down from 139. Not sure why my TC dropped so much...don't I need that higher to help with the hormonies?

I'm thinking my iron defficiency anemia and leaky gut are wreaking havoc on my rT3. My blood sugar is normally in the 95 area which is still high but is way high on here. I'm not sure what came first...the chicken or the egg? I'm guessing the first things to address are the Iron and the leaky gut. Then the Insulin resistance? I'm thinking the leaky gut is throwing off the cholesterol and thyroid. What to do first?

I'm confused about the Antibodies. I've read some conflicting things and other than the rT3 the others seem to be within normal functional ranges but what specifically is considered optimal? I've been to STTM and my head is kind of spinning.

Any help would be much appreciated!

ETA: Oh ya DHEA & Sex hormones are in the TOILET!  But what is optimal?

>
>
>
> ETA: Oh ya DHEA & Sex hormones are in the TOILET!  But what is optimal?

I am not a doctor.

....

low iron----->Gluten Free Diet

high fasting glucose---->Metformin + Cycloset

Sex Horm Bind Glob 21.6 nmol/L 24.6-122.0 LOW------->very low, many problems

goal DHEAs----Women 275-400 μg/dL----->use 100mg/day 7ketoDHEA then as much DHEA as it takes

All labs done on approx CD 22 of 30. \*33 yo F

Progesterone 4.4 ng/mL------->should be 14

Estradiol 98.7 pg/mL ---->should be 140

missing a lot on progesterone

Try to align your P&E on (day 2-4) and day (19-22)

Start with day (2-4)

monitor your steroid hormones using blood tests, do it preferably at LabCorp, use this list:

1 ------- Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140

2 ------- 17α-Hydroxypregnenolone by HPLC-MS/MS

3 ------- 17α-Hydroxyprogesterone by HPLC-MS/MS

4 ------- Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144

5 ------- Cortisol, Free, Serum (Free Cortisol, Serum Cortisol, CBG)

6 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM Test Number: 104000 CPT Code: 82533(x2) (7:30AM, 12PM)

7 ------- Corticosteroid-binding Globulin (CBG), Transcortin

8 ------- Corticotropin Releasing Hormone (CRH)

9 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

10 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA)--Test Number: 004100 CPT Code: 82626

11 ------- Androstenedione, LCMS--Test Number: 004705 CPT Code: 82157

12 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum Test Number: 004226 CPT Code: 84403

13 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound--Test Number: 143255 CPT Code: 84402; 84403

14 ------- SHBG

15 ------- Dihydrotestosterone (DHT)--Test Number: 500142 CPT Code: 82651

16 ------- Androstanediol Glucuronide (Endocrine Sciences) Test Number: 500881 CPT Code: 82154

17 ------- Estrogens, Total; Test Number: 004549 CPT Code: 82672

18 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670

19 ------- Estrone, Serum Test Number: 004564 CPT Code: 82679

Your pregnenolone is likely low.

Convince your doc to:

Start adjustments of DHEAs first (it accounts for 95% of major steroid hormones by weight)

Next use

pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricilogy/Allergy Research, 150mg tabs

do not be surprised if you end up using 1000mg/day

buy it at HerbsPro.com buy by dozen it cost less

After you tested, and actually find out that your pregnenolone is indeed low,

it looks like good starting dose for you may be 600mg/day, split, wakeup(50-70%), balance 3-4 hrs latter or split in 3 progressively lower doses, last one around 2PM, first you may take on your trip to bathroom if it is 1-2 hrs before wakeup.

You want to use enough pregnenolone to

be high on range (priority #3)

to (hopefully) rise progesterone to desirable level (priority #2)

to (hopefully get just right the Cortisol-7:30AM ~20)

patience is need, you have all buckets empty of hormones, takes time to fill them in, you have to titrate dose over number of cycles.

Make a good will effort to not use progesterone supplementation.

\------

get TSH

assume that your pituitary works ok (no tumors)

if TSH is high

use Natural Thyroid (I use Thyroid-S bought at Amazon)

Use 1 or 2 no more than 3 tablets, one tablet=1Grain

aim at TSH(0.5-1.5)

Keep waiting for the whole body to work thru all these changes (probably about a year)

Asses where you stand.

If you have a means do this two tests, scan each page, post results on this thread.

I will figure for you list of supplements that should benefit you.

Do not eat fish oils, do not eat krill oils, do not eat any any supplemental oils

unless you tested blood and know what you actually need.

.

2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)

1 dropperfull/day

Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles Price: $73.90

[http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)

monitor board info on iodine supplementation, (SSKI may need to be used instead of Lugol's, wait for dr Kruses answer to my post #54

low iron----->Gluten Free Diet

high fasting glucose---->Metformin + Cycloset

goal DHEAs----Women 275-400 μg/dL----->use 100mg/day 7ketoDHEA then as much DHEA as it takes

missing a lot on progesterone

Try to align your P&E on (day 2-4) and day (19-22)

Start with day (2-4)

monitor your steroid hormones using blood tests, do it preferably at LabCorp, use this list:

1 ------- Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140

2 ------- 17α-Hydroxypregnenolone by HPLC-MS/MS

3 ------- 17α-Hydroxyprogesterone by HPLC-MS/MS

4 ------- Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144

5 ------- Cortisol, Free, Serum (Free Cortisol, Serum Cortisol, CBG)

6 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM Test Number: 104000 CPT Code: 82533(x2) (7:30AM, 12PM)

7 ------- Corticosteroid-binding Globulin (CBG), Transcortin

8 ------- Corticotropin Releasing Hormone (CRH)

9 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

10 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA)--Test Number: 004100 CPT Code: 82626

11 ------- Androstenedione, LCMS--Test Number: 004705 CPT Code: 82157

12 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum Test Number: 004226 CPT Code: 84403

13 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound--Test Number: 143255 CPT Code: 84402; 84403

14 ------- SHBG

15 ------- Dihydrotestosterone (DHT)--Test Number: 500142 CPT Code: 82651

16 ------- Androstanediol Glucuronide (Endocrine Sciences) Test Number: 500881 CPT Code: 82154

17 ------- Estrogens, Total; Test Number: 004549 CPT Code: 82672

18 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive Test Number: 140244 CPT Code: 82670

19 ------- Estrone, Serum Test Number: 004564 CPT Code: 82679

Your pregnenolone is likely low.

Convince your doc to:

Start adjustments of DHEAs first (it accounts for 95% of major steroid hormones by weight)

Next use

pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricilogy/Allergy Research, 150mg tabs

do not be surprised if you end up using 1000mg/day

buy it at HerbsPro.com buy by dozen it cost less

After you tested, and actually find out that your pregnenolone is indeed low,

it looks like good starting dose for you may be 600mg/day, split, wakeup(50-70%), balance 3-4 hrs latter or split in 3 progressively lower doses, last one around 2PM, first you may take on your trip to bathroom if it is 1-2 hrs before wakeup.

You want to use enough pregnenolone to

be high on range (priority #3)

to (hopefully) rise progesterone to desirable level (priority #2)

to (hopefully get just right the Cortisol-7:30AM ~20)

patience is need, you have all buckets empty of hormones, takes time to fill them in, you have to titrate dose over number of cycles.

Make a good will effort to not use progesterone supplementation.

\------

get TSH

assume that your pituitary works ok (no tumors)

if TSH is high

use Natural Thyroid (I use Thyroid-S bought at Amazon)

aim at TSH(0.5-1.5)

Keep waiting for the whole body to work thru all these changes (probably about a year)

Hi JanSz-

Thanks for your input. I'll look @ this more closely when I get home from work. I've been gluten/grain free for at least 1.5 years. I eat seafood ~ 1 meal/day since that is all I can afford right now. Sauerkraut w/ most meals, yogurt, CO/butter/tallow on all my meats that are pretty much all Grassfed/finished. I was supplementing with 5000 IU Vit D/ day but Ive cut that down to 2000 since getting these results. May actually go back to 5000 since heading into winter. Have a consult w/ Dr. K soon so will see what he says on multiple fronts.

I'm thinking my leaky gut is somehow inhibiting my iron absorption which is throwing off thyroid, LDL, etc. I eat more red meat than anything & my levels are still severely low.

I've been taking 100mg 7keto DHEA + 100mg DHEA twice a day, once in AM w/ BAB and the other with lunch. It sounds like I need much more but will see what once I have my consult.

Other than iron supplements with Vit C is there any other way to up my iron levels? I don't want to take too much since Iron is supposed to be so toxic when high. I may have enough from foods but just not absorbing it...so then what? So many moving parts!

I'm also thinking too many berries this summer has upped my FBG. My weight steadily increased from spring too, finally starting to go the other way. I don't think summer and I are good for one another at this time. It's not you, it's me...I think we need to break up until I'm healthy by staying cold. . Winter is comming!

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-09-17T21:06:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-gut-12-is-now-live.3684/post-52184>

I will comment here, where I can write easier than in the blog's comments .

It seems that I have always to disagree... in the immaterial details . [The GULO mutation](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L-gulonolactone_oxidaseRIPTION-%21) took place a lot of time previously to our brain evolution and other animals (like guinea pigs) with it haven't developed bigger brains. Nevertheless, it seems strange two so unlikely traits happening on the same species (big brain and GULO mutation) so it seems to me more likely that the GULO mutation was required for the speeding up of virus and bacterial (zonulin?) ADN uptake by our ancestors: with an steady production of ascorbic acid it wouldn't have taken place so quickly, it seems to me.

The problem with my line of thought is that [cetaceans](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cetacean_intelligence) doesn't seem to fit, almost. I have only been able to find [a very lateral reference](http://http://www.jwildlifedis.org/content/11/4/460.full.pdf) to a lower ascorbic acid production in livers of cetaceans ([this other reference](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0300962980900067)[http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0300962980900067](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0300962980900067) looks promising, but I don't have access):

> Miller and Ridgway reported cases of necrotic stomatitis in dolphins that responded to ascorbic acid therapy, and the authors considered this stomatitis a form of scurvy.

Since vertebrates develop ascorbic acid production when becoming _land/fresh water_ dwellers due to iodine shortage (I can't find the reference to this hypothesis now, but there are several about fresh water fishes with ascorbic acid capability like [this one](http://http://ecology.zjnu.edu.cn/sts/xiezg/pdf/CBP.pdf)[http://http://ecology.zjnu.edu.cn/sts/xiezg/pdf/CBP.pdf](http://http://ecology.zjnu.edu.cn/sts/xiezg/pdf/CBP.pdf)), it seems plausible that cetacean decreased its production when they came back to sea life and its iodine abundance.

On the other side, your blog has given some clues about a little mistery. My father (77 years old) had several **sebaceous cysts on his head (again, they had to cut them off some 30 years ago) that have disappeared** spontaneously after the intake of several supplements for COPD (chronic occlusive pulmonary disease) and BPH (benign prostate hyperplasia): vitamins D, C, K2 and E (with tocotrienols), NAC, magnesium and iodine/iodide (very low dose, 225mcg until recently, slowly increasing it). I had no idea which one of them had been responsible until now, although I am a little doubtful of so small a dose having an effect. Nevertheless, we will keep upping our iodine/iodide dose .

### skline@peak.org — 2012-09-17T21:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-gut-12-is-now-live.3684/post-52194>

Jack, you go from strength to strength.

Independent minds work alike -- I had already decided to print out all of your posts, so I could read them under a warm-colored light instead of staring into a video screen for hours and hours.

By the way, sweet potatoes and people with oxalate troubles should not mix. In the aftermath, I'm off all dairy, caffeine (even decaf coffee), nightshades, beans, chocolate, and wine, (already long off all grains), and I'm eating more fish. Gut problems seem to take a long time to resolve, but I'm making progress. Eating fresh fruit only in season and local, mostly small fruit from my yard, except for squeezing some lemon juice into my drinking water. For small amounts of non-standard fruit, the yard is easy, as things come ripe and then finish one after another. This time of year, Cornelian cherry dogwood fruit (falls off when ripe enough), some nearly dry blueberries, absolutely delicious, Himalayan blackberries as I chop them out, a few blue muscat grapes (rest will be mashed and turned to vinegar), and then in October "autumn olive berries" (an eleagnus, soft red berries full of lycopene), with medlars close behind, which will last nearly till Christmas. because they have to "ret" on the counter after the first frost. Then nothing but a few frozen blueberries and a little lemon juice, till next year.

Glad you're here.

I have only been able to find [a very lateral reference](http://http://www.jwildlifedis.org/content/11/4/460.full.pdf) to a lower ascorbic acid production in livers of cetaceans ([this other reference](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0300962980900067)[http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0300962980900067](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/0300962980900067) looks promising, but I don't have access):
>
>
>
> Since vertebrates develop ascorbic acid production when becoming _land/fresh water_ dwellers due to iodine shortage (I can't find the reference to this hypothesis now, but there are several about fresh water fishes with ascorbic acid capability like [this one](http://http://ecology.zjnu.edu.cn/sts/xiezg/pdf/CBP.pdf)[http://http://ecology.zjnu.edu.cn/sts/xiezg/pdf/CBP.pdf](http://http://ecology.zjnu.edu.cn/sts/xiezg/pdf/CBP.pdf)), it seems plausible that cetacean decreased its production when they came back to sea life and its iodine abundance.
>

>
> On the other side, your blog has given some clues about a little mistery. My father (77 years old) had several **sebaceous cysts on his head (again, they had to cut them off some 30 years ago) that have **disappeared spontaneously after the intake of several supplements for COPD (chronic occlusive pulmonary disease) and BPH (benign prostate hyperplasia): vitamins D, C, K2 and E (with tocotrienols), NAC, magnesium and iodine/iodide (very low dose, 225mcg until recently, slowly increasing it). I had no idea which one of them had been responsible until now, although I am a little doubtful of so small a dose having an effect. Nevertheless, we will keep upping our iodine/iodide dose .******

******

If humans, other haplorrhines, and guinea pigs share common ancestry:

Since they all would have once biosynthesized L-ascorbic acid, we should find the the GULO gene and all the remaining genes in the pathway. **And we do.**

Deactivation:

We should find deactivating mutations, which are mutations that render the GULO gene incapable of producing the GULO enzyme, or even activating in the first place, i.e. a GULO pseudogene (GULOP). And we do.**

If the deactivation occurred in an ancestor common to the haplorrhines, we should expect to find the same deactivating mutation in all haplorrhine GULOPs. And we do**.

Since there are many other species genetically closer to haplorrhines than guinea pigs, and since those species have active GULOs, the guinea pig GULOP deactivation should be lineage-specific; so we should expect to find its deactivating mutation in a different location. And we do.**

Since pseudogenes accumulate mutations from generation to generation, and since the haplorrhine GULOP would have to have been in pseudogene form across many speciation events (and in turn, many common ancestors), we should find shared mutations (again, manifesting as shared SNPs), and we should find that they are arranged in such a way that they give an unbroken line of inheritance for every species (a nested hierarchy). **And we do.

**_Moreover, If they never shared common ancestry and were separately designed:_

Since they would have been designed to lack the capability of biosynthesizing L-ascorbic acid, we should not find the genes for the entire pathway. **Yet we do.

Deactivation:

Despite that we should not find GULO in the first place, if present, we should find deactivating mutations, i.e. GULOP. **And we do.**

If the deactivation was designed into these separate creations, we should expect to find either the same deactivating mutation in every GULOP, or an arbitrary, disordered distribution of shared and unshared deactivating mutations. Yet we find neither.**

If they don't share common ancestry, since the majority of GULOP deactivation are in the same location, we should find that the guinea pig GULOP deactivation is likewise in the same location as it is in haplorrhines. Yet we do not.**

Since they are separate creations, we should find little to no shared SNPs, and we should find that whatever few did arise are not arranged in any particular pattern. Yet we do not.**

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-09-17T23:22:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-gut-12-is-now-live.3684/post-52210>

My fault: "GULO **inactivating mutation**s**". I can think quite straight, so I am not a creationist by any means (I wouldn't be wearing my DIY ice-vest right now if I wouldn't think that cold is in the core of eutherians' success, thanks to you). Nevertheless, the point stands: haplorrhines' mutation happened a lot of time before our brains' evolution. Perhaps it is a needed but not sufficient condition to speedy brain development through your gut permeability hypothesis (less ascorbic acid, more viruses, more _junk_ DNA, speedier adaptation), but I can't see its opposing relation to iodine:

> Jack Kruse wrote:

**

> Yes, the evolution of the human brain may be the reason Vitamin C lost its biologic mojo in our species.

I know there is a piece of advice to not mix iodine/iodide with vitamin C intake, but in [those expectant mothers taking high vitamin C doses it doesn't seem to have impaired their babies' brain development](http://http://www.doctoryourself.com/miscarriage.html) (ok, that's not very high quality evidence, but it is what there is available, and I have quite high respect for clinical experience like Klenner's, Cathcart's or, for that matter, yours).

It may happen that I am misunderstanding you again, though, since I may be inferring causality in your words that isn't there.

The infant brain is quite immature.......I would not expect vitamin C to do much to it at all. But if you are iodine deficient while that brain matures over the first few months you become a cretin!!! Giving the same child vitamin C will not stop the development of cretinism........what does that tell you about Vitamin C? It does nothing for the neural synapses oxidation protection. If Vitamin C is that good it should have a protective effect for synapses......or the neurons for that matter and it does not. This is why I think vitamin C was not naturally selected for in humans. Our ancestors were arborial and ate boatloads of vitamin C in fruit......and they had rudimentary brains with not too many cortical layers......Vitamin C is big for them. Iodine is not........humans evolved in an iodine rich environment and not one in the trees or savanna. BG 4 shows you the geoology of the cradle of humanity. Evolutionary selection is form follows function and older systems under go exaptation.

What do you think?

Jack

### x_danny_x — 2012-09-16T02:27:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-carbs-are-you-supposed-to-eat-during-the-fall-also-bacon-sodium-question.3686/post-51881>

I know in winter time, Carb should be around 0, ketogenic diet time. But what about the fall? Do you start to gradually decrease your carb intake bit by bit until it reaches 0 and it is winter time?

And for the Bacon, well generally sodium. How much sodium are we supposed to take in a day? I like to eat alot of bacon but I am worried about the amount of sodium that I am taking in daily. Bacon has a high amount of sodium.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-16T02:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-carbs-are-you-supposed-to-eat-during-the-fall-also-bacon-sodium-question.3686/post-51883>

I dont worry about sodium unless I have a kidney issue........you eat the carbs that normally grow at your latitude and longitude for that time of the yr.......here is the best part......most modern humans have no clue what that is.......and that is why they are not rockstarish.

> right now, i am listening to your 1 hour appearance with Shawn Croxton in Underground Wellness, lol.

Danny,

I'm just below you in PA. We have similar growing seasons and I have a great friend that farms in upstate NY. In Fall, you can still easily grow lots of brassicas like cabbage, kale, broccoli, cauliflower and from fall through winter you should still be able to find local spinach, lettuce and swiss chard. They are both pretty cold tolerant and I can grow them in my little home garden all through winter.

This is all in addition to the winter squashes (butternut, pumpkin, hubbard, kabocha, etc) that Gretchen mentioned above.
