# Thread: newbie from Va with Hashimoto's
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### renwoo — 2012-09-17T05:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52061>

Hello all,

I am very intrigued by everything I have been reading on this site but I also feel like I need an advanced degree to understand some of this! LOL I was hoping for some guidance from some more experienced folks. I am 40 yo woman and have Hashimotos and have been taking synthroid for about 6-7 years. I also have almost zero vit d levels (17 at last blood work) and now take 50,000 2x a week. I am very overweight, exhausted, thinning hair, crazy heavy periods that come early, moody and irritated. I am very interested in making a big shift in my life regarding my health, and I would love to get off synthroid. Of course I need to lose a huge amount of weight, I am 5ft 4 and 215lbs. I tend to be all or nothing and then revert back to my crap eating...because I get overwhelmed and honestly I read too much and then I don't know 'who to trust' so to speak. I need to get the inertia to make the shift...Ironically I own and have read "Make S#it Happen" Great read... So I ask where do I begin? Anyone with Hashimoto's have any success getting off meds? Help! Thank you for reading

### caroline — 2012-09-17T11:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52080>

Hi renwoo and welcome - no question - there is a lot of reading! You kind of have to keep reading Dr.K's blogs over and over until it soaks in. It will take time and there are lots of smart and kind people here to answer your questions. I think the biggest thing is to know that you have to take charge of your own health. It is a lot of hard work but you can do it. Have you thought of having a consultation with Dr. Kruse? there are a few different ways to do it and you can read about people's experiences. There are alot of people here with Hashis. Dr. K. is very supportive of everyone and is involved on this forum every day - we frankly often wonder if he ever sleeps! Have you checked the easy start guide? Have a look at it and just start!! It won't cost you anything except time and it could save your life. good luck and keep posting to let us know how you are doing - maybe start a journal - about your road to optimum. This forum has been a life changer for a lot of us - but make no mistake - it is hard work and won't happen overnite. Dr. K. always tells us - we didn't get broken overnite so don't expect to get fixed overnite. Have a look at the journals and you can follow people's journey and see all their ups and downs and struggle with balancing hormones or whatever their problem is and fitting all this into their daily life. I can promise you - if you put in the work - you won't look back!

### mehreen — 2012-09-17T12:00:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52081>

Very nice ,

thanks a lot.

### Dali Dula — 2012-09-17T14:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52092>

here are my recommendations

1\. Read the leptin RX and implement. You should start feeling better soon.

2\. Get labs if you can. If your T3 is low it needs to be addressed. Synthroid only addresses T4. Post your labs as Hack My Labs. You will get help here, but it is not medical advice. It is interpretation to take to your doctor. Like most here your hormones are probably trashed. I have been here almost a year and I'm just getting started on the hormone situation.

3\. Start reading the blog. It is dense. Read the comments.

4\. After learning more schedule a consult with Dr. K. Record it if you can. The amount of info in these consults is amazing and you will want to review it.

You have found a great site that can change your life.

### renwoo — 2012-09-17T16:28:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52099>

Hi all,

Thank you for the replies and encouragement. My last labs were in July and my Endocrinologist doesn't test my T3, he talks 90 mph and every time I bring anything off his course up he tells me 'monkey's are on the internet, can't trust anything'...in other words...shut up and do as I tell you. I am embarassed to admit that until recently I didn't know Hashi peeps are supposed to avoid soy/gluten/etc...  I was reading late last night in here and learned about SSKI and was amazed...I have sebaceous cysts all over my scalp that various drs have attempted to take out with minimal success and no one has ever told why I get them...they would say 'oh it just happens to some' I am not a fan of that vague answers. I am the kind of person that really needs, wants to, HAS to understand what is going on. I ordered some SSKI last night. Should I post my July labs? Where would I do that?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-17T17:57:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52124>

I already responded to the labs.......you need to get to LEF.org and begin to order the ones the endo wont order so you understand what needs to be done and you are not told what will be done.

### MartiD — 2012-09-17T18:07:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-from-va-with-hashimotos.3706/post-52127>

As a new Hashi's patient I totally understand your frustration with your Endo. My suggestion is to find a new Dr. I am on my 3rd! Also, if you go on a strict Auto Immune diet which excludes all gluten, soy, sugar, eggs, dairy and nuts, I would imagine you will start to feel the effects that that very quickly. I would so implement the Leptin RX program with the AI diet. It isn't easy and it won't fix things over night but if you are eating alot of gluten you will see the effects of cutting that out soon! First week may be rough with the detox but trust me it is worth it! Good luck!

# Thread: Bulletproof Coffee/Vanilla and Coffeeamakers/Accessories

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2012-09-17T10:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52077>

I recently bought some Upgraded Coffee to try and I have to say I like it. Using the butter and coconut oil in coffee. They say you need six tablespoons of coconut oil to equal the same amount of MCT oil...but that seems like too much so I don't do that.

I'm using a 5 cup programmable Mr. Coffee which a reviewer said used 170 degree water. Is this not hot enough to make good coffee? Also does anyone know the temperature that starts to cause issues with Vanilla? They mention at Bulletproof Exec site that Vanilla loses benefits if its heated too much but they say its good in Bulletproof Coffee...so how hot is too much...do I need to wait till it cools before putting in Vanilla?

I haven't bought the Vanilla yet but would like some kind of flavor and Vanilla seems to have some good health benefits from my reading so far. Creamers like Irish cream and such would be great but they have sugar...not that much but I should probably avoid it. Would it be possible to get an Irish Cream flavor without added sugar and bad oil in standard liquid creamer? Also what exactly is French Vanilla...is it Vanilla with something else? Possible to get that flavor without junk?

Any recommendations on a good Coffee maker and/or blender for Bulletproof Coffee would be great. I currently just stir with a spoon.

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-17T14:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52090>

I have a Miletta that I snagged off amazon for $40. It's great. I've also got some French presses too ( coffee snob here)

When I make coffee. I measure out 2 Cups and use 3TBS of coffee grounds

I use just a little CO w/some Kerry gold

Fair warning I like my coffee strong & rich.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-17T16:51:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52103>

Coffee snob here too.

I have a Technivorm Moccamaster - the temp thing IS important for coffee snobbery! It's quite pricey for a coffee maker, but for us this is an appliance I use multiple times a day.

Or, you could become a client of mine - I give them as gifts to my coffee drinkers! Sell your TX house and move to CO!

Might I suggest considering making your own vanilla? So easy to do, makes great gifts - and it's a world of difference!

You can order beans by the 1/2 pd or pound from here (this is my place):

[http://www.ebay.com/itm/30-Organic-Gourmet-Grade-A-Madagascar-Bourbon-Vanilla-Beans-6-7-/230765013510?pt=LH\_DefaultDomain\_0&hash=item35baaaaa06](http://http://www.ebay.com/itm/30-Organic-Gourmet-Grade-A-Madagascar-Bourbon-Vanilla-Beans-6-7-/230765013510?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_0&hash=item35baaaaa06)

I do batches of both madagascar and tahijian!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-09-17T17:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52105>

> **Raiken3712;52694 wrote:** I recently bought some Upgraded Coffee to try and I have to say I like it. Using the butter and coconut oil in coffee. They say you need six tablespoons of coconut oil to equal the same amount of MCT oil...but that seems like too much so I don't do that.
>
>
>
> I'm using a 5 cup programmable Mr. Coffee which a reviewer said used 170 degree water. Is this not hot enough to make good coffee? Also does anyone know the temperature that starts to cause issues with Vanilla? They mention at Bulletproof Exec site that Vanilla loses benefits if its heated too much but they say its good in Bulletproof Coffee...so how hot is too much...do I need to wait till it cools before putting in Vanilla?
>
>
>
> I haven't bought the Vanilla yet but would like some kind of flavor and Vanilla seems to have some good health benefits from my reading so far. Creamers like Irish cream and such would be great but they have sugar...not that much but I should probably avoid it. Would it be possible to get an Irish Cream flavor without added sugar and bad oil in standard liquid creamer? Also what exactly is French Vanilla...is it Vanilla with something else? Possible to get that flavor without junk?
>
>
>
> Any recommendations on a good Coffee maker and/or blender for Bulletproof Coffee would be great. I currently just stir with a spoon.

Hi Raiken3712,

I bought the 5lbs of Bulletproof coffee for my cold thermogenesis experiment. I wasn't impressed by the coffee. First it was harsh and bitter but adding heavy cream and lots of butter removed all these negative flavors. I have been converted over to putting butter in my coffee though. I wonder if it is worth the extra cost. The price even before shipping is a few dollars a pound over a certified organic coffee. The whole mycotoxins thing might be of importance to someone with a allergy to fungus but for the rest of us, I think it's just a unique marketing angle. I don't think Bulletproof coffee improved my mental abilities better than an equal amount of a quality certified organic coffee. Any coffee increases mental function and especially focus.

If I were you, I would add my vanilla or a vanilla extract after the coffee is made. You could try grinding your own organic vanilla beans or made a vanilla extract with an organic vodka. Consider making your own vanilla extract. It is very easy and once you have make vanilla extract it opens the door to making your own extract medicines:

[http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-comfort-for-colds/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-comfort-for-colds/)

[http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-staying-clean-safely-and-saving-money/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/healthy-household-staying-clean-safely-and-saving-money/)

It is best to grind your own coffee beans fresh each day and store the coffee beans in the freezer. As for a coffee maker, I use a stove top stainless steel expresso maker. I also think those glass Bodum presses are very good. Protect your health from plastics and use nothing but stainless steel or glass:

[http://bialettishop.com/EspressoMakerMainPageStainlessSteel.htm](http://http://bialettishop.com/EspressoMakerMainPageStainlessSteel.htm)

[http://www.bodum.com/ca/en-us/shop/prodlist/262/](http://http://www.bodum.com/ca/en-us/shop/prodlist/262/)

Be careful with stimulate use. After my two months of cold thermogenesis experiment I quit the coffee. I find coffee is hard on my endocrine system and I find myself overstimulated by coffee. I am closing in on peri-menopause and my endocrine system does not need the extra stress of the drug caffeine.

I said earlier, any coffee will increase mental function and especially focus. This is why coffee is the drug of choice for North America. Coffee is great for getting us workers to focus on work. If course, we never look up to see if the work is worth doing. If coffee was more introspective it wouldn't be the favorite drug of industry and business. Coffee can use up your youth and vitality and leave you an empty shell of a person. That's what happens when your endocrine system crashes and burns up from long term drug use. I wish I had quit years ago.

Caroline there was a post in the bulletproof blog by a BC person who researched local coffees that fit the parameters for bulletproof status. I've tried a couple of these and have been happiest with the Doi Chaan Single Estate dark roast available at London Drugs for about $16/bag. if you don't like dark roast there is also a medium roast.

others on the list were:

49th Parallel (available at some Save-On More Stores, this is a very light roast even the espresso is pale)

mogiana (I've seen this at WF and some farmer's markets)

elysian coffee roasters (did not like this one, got it at WF)

origins coffee

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-09-17T19:51:55+0300

>
> elysian coffee roasters (did not like this one, got it at WF)

**

The type of coffee I used before quitting coffee was **Kicking Horse certified organic **Kick Ass blend:

[http://kickinghorsecoffee.com/store/product\_info.php?products\_id=75&language=Fr](http://http://kickinghorsecoffee.com/store/product_info.php?products_id=75&language=Fr)

Presently, I use a certified organic matcha** from Mountain Rose Herbs** which I really like but is no substitute for a good cup of coffee. This matcha is the best price I have found by the pound:

[http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/tea\_bev/tea\_greenwhite.html](http://http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/tea_bev/tea_greenwhite.html)

If I am feeling like I want coffee during this withdrawal phase I use hot water with straight certified organic cocoa powder**. No sweetener. It is a nice bitter drink like coffee. It is also good with a few tablespoons of heavy raw cream and pasture butter. I don't like using cocoa powder for long term because cocoa powder will bother my digestion over time. (Another damn "bean" I can't tolerate. I don't know why people think plants are safe.)

Ahh! You just connected some dots for me...NO! I didn't even 'think' about coffee (and cocoa) as 'beans'. It's so easy to not eat beans of any other sort b/c a) they taste gross and b) I get immediate feedback from my digestion to not eat them...

I see why I've seen some comments where Jack recommends against coffee for some ppl. It didn't even seem like it was directly because of the caffeine either. Ok...I will work to limit my coffee intake...ouch. I don't feel dependent on it...but it REALLY tastes good though. hehe I usually only drink it in the morning though and NEVER after 2pm (just as a rule).

Perhaps if I'm disciplined enough I will experiment NO COFFEE for the month of October! I will admit this one would be a bit more difficult for me, since it's considered a 'treat' for me. I need to shift my mentality on this one as I have done successfully regarding every other protocol. hehe.

### cookworm — 2012-09-20T05:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52863>

Is cold brewing for the occasional coffee okay for paleo?

I enjoy cold brewed coffee (they do sell machines to make cold brewed coffee, but it's not necessary). I use 1/2 tablespoon per 8 ounces of water, put it in a glass jar and stir to combine, then let it sit in the fridge overnight, then strain out the grounds the next day (some make their cold brewed coffee strong and dilute it when drinking; I just make it as strong as I normally enjoy it and don't dilute it). I use coconut milk and coconut oil in my coffee and tea.

### Raiken3712 — 2012-09-20T06:27:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52866>

Do people usually preheat the butter/coconut oil? It lowers the temperature of the coffee a bunch when I put in refrigerated butter and the coconut oil.

### cookworm — 2012-09-20T19:41:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52943>

> **Raiken3712;53498 wrote:** Do people usually preheat the butter/coconut oil? It lowers the temperature of the coffee a bunch when I put in refrigerated butter and the coconut oil.

Instead of butter, I use coconut milk that's refrigerated. While it does cool down my coffee quite a bit, I actually prefer the lukewarm temperature the cold coconut milk gives it rather than the scalding hot coffee (now, ask me in winter if I'll feel the same way!) I don't heat up the coconut oil either, but I don't notice if the coconut oil itself lowers the temp of my coffee (I do store mine at room temperature, though).

### Raiken3712 — 2012-09-20T19:44:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-52946>

From the reviews of my coffee maker they say its around 170 which isn't really all that hot. I don't like scalding hot either but I like the temperature it comes out. Its drinkable at that temp. Also I've heard higher temperatures are better...not sure if that's for extracting the good stuff out of the coffee or just as a taste thing?

>
> I enjoy cold brewed coffee (they do sell machines to make cold brewed coffee, but it's not necessary). I use 1/2 tablespoon per 8 ounces of water, put it in a glass jar and stir to combine, then let it sit in the fridge overnight, then strain out the grounds the next day (some make their cold brewed coffee strong and dilute it when drinking; I just make it as strong as I normally enjoy it and don't dilute it). I use coconut milk and coconut oil in my coffee and tea.**

**

Hi cookworm,

Another SCDer! I have been on the diet for years. It cured 90% of my problems!

One note about coconut milk. It is really hard to find a good one. The additives in most coconut milk such as guar gum are "illegal" on the Specific Carbohydrate Diet. Here are some of the problems with industrial coconut milk even the organic stuff:

[http://chriskresser.com/3-reasons-why-coconut-milk-may-not-be-your-friend](http://http://chriskresser.com/3-reasons-why-coconut-milk-may-not-be-your-friend)

In this post in the comment section I talk about how to make your own coconut milk. It's very easy and costs a lot less than buying the product in a canned form:

[http://eatkamloops.org/brazil-coconut-candy/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/brazil-coconut-candy/)

I noted that you have an AI condition. Have you tried removing the Nightshade family plants from your diet? About 1/3 of the population has a sensitivity to Nightshades. I have a partial sensitivity to Nightshade. (Many people on the SCD/GAPS diet note an improvement of osteoarthritis after cutting out the potatoes. This happened to me.) Here is a excellent essay from the **Weston A Price Foundation** on Nightshades**:

[http://www.westonaprice.org/food-features/nightshades](http://http://www.westonaprice.org/food-features/nightshades)

By the way, my osteoarthritis completely went away. The only time I have problems with joint pain is this time of the year. I eat lots of fresh Nightshade plants: tomatoes, peppers and eggplants. It reminds me of the "bad" olde days! I have cut out all the fresh tomatoes. I don't touch potatoes or I will be in pain for about three days. Cooked tomatoes seem okay for me but as I age my sensitives may increase. I am watching this situation closely because I do not want to go back to living with chronic pain.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-22T21:25:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bulletproof-coffee-vanilla-and-coffeeamakers-accessories.3708/post-53452>

> **Patty Cakes;53595 wrote:** I use room temp ghee whenever I have BP coffee or brewed chocolate.

Wait, don't you have hashi's? You are drinking coffee and chocolate? I thought it was a no-no!

I think I need a new Endo after reading some of this site...I realize that he is not even checking my T3...thank you for your input! ### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-17T17:50:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-here-please-hack-my-labs.3711/post-52120>

There is not enough to go on here. But your TG need to get better by eating better. Your GLc is solid but only a one time measure. You need a HS cRP and Vita D to see if your TSH is useful or not......my bet it is not. And I think a rev T3 would show how well the synthroid really is. you need a SHBG, free T3, rev T3 and a lot of other things to really assess how good bad or ugly things are. You really need to read BG 5, 6, 9, 11, and 12 a lot............then you may begin to see why this is all important.

Do not, do not eat fish oils or krill oil. (More complicated than that).

No exercising, or very very minor.

Eating 1+ pounds/day mix of oysters, scallops, muscles, shrimps

olive oil, coconut oil, clarified butter (ghee)

and

drink green tea

aim for 30% proteins, 70% fat (by calories)

\-----------------------------------

Circadian Rhythm maintenance:

Sleep in legal dark room, within 10PM - 8AM period

Eat 50% of your daily food within first 30min after wkeup

Exposure you whole body to the best bright sunlight you can arrange, there are some discussions about how good is high intensity artificial light (when you are not able to arrange sunlight).

lunch may be, may be not

dinner, within 4-6 hours of bed time

no snacking

no artificial lights after sundown

use flux.com program on your computer screen, amber filter, better do not use any electronic devices.

\--------------------------------------

### renwoo — 2012-09-17T20:06:17+0300

This was my yearly checkup, my vit d is at the bottom its 17.5. He didn't like that and upped my synthroid I go in for an ultrasound and new labs in Nov. ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-17T20:11:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-here-please-hack-my-labs.3711/post-52168>

I honestly don't think he has ever measured T3, much less the others listed. I might have to find a new doctor.... How can I eat within 30 min if your supposed to wait 60 after synthroid?? Oh wow, and how do you get around not liking seafood?? This is all so foreign...Why no exercising? Thank you both for responding, wow this is all very new info to learn...I will go and read your blogs and get out my medical dictionary and paper to take notes  thank you!

Renwoo, please give yourself some time to read Jack's blogs. So much in there will help you.

MOST IMPORTANTLY, there is no "might" about it. Find a new doc. 1st, look up other endo's and make at least 3 appointments and go interview them. I know this is so hard to do - but you must, this guy is not helping you. You are better off doing it yourself with info on here than what he is doing.

When you have a lot of things to fix, you have to triage and make sure you take care of snagging the low hanging fruit - biggest thing is circadian cycles. . .then not eating damaging foods, then eating more good foods,and on down the line.

Sometimes getting lost in the details and giving up is a way of avoidance. So what if you are not perfect on one thing way down the list when you start? You will find over time, these things - are easier done than said.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-17T20:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brand-new-here-please-hack-my-labs.3711/post-52178>

> **Cavemam;52785 wrote:** MOST IMPORTANTLY, there is no "might" about it. Find a new doc. 1st, look up other endo's and make at least 3 appointments and go interview them. I know this is so hard to do - but you must, this guy is not helping you. You are better off doing it yourself with info on here than what he is doing.

I agree on you finding a new doc. I my personal experience, I couldn't find an endo to help. I called up several when I was looking for thyroid help and just asked the receptionist if he scripts natural thyroid or T3 (natural compounded T3 even better). They always said no so I went on to find a doc with thyroid experience and it turned out to be a hormone specialist. Use the phone to help as an interview method and save time and money. Once you have the docs you want to visit in person at least you will have the preliminaries out of the way.

Also agree on the reading here. I know it seems overwhelming but try to digest what you can. And go back and reread certain ones several times. I still do that and get new info from the blogs each time. I have Hashis too and it is a pain to deal with and all the ramifications that come from it. But know you can be better than you are. Lots to do but well worth it! Start somewhere and you will already be further along than most! Good luck and enjoy the journey along the way...it's quite a ride!

But if the iodine/DMSO combination works on sebaceous cysts, perhaps it will work quickly on my cyst. I'm interested in trying.
> Where does one buy medical grade DMSO?

Old thread, I've bought DMSO on Vitacost in recent years - not sure of the grade. I used know of DMSO years ago related to horses for leg lineament but I had fears over it because my Daddy used it for his severe shoulder pain. He got his from treatment place in Mexico back in the mid-70's and my Mother believes that DMSO was responsible for the begining of Daddy's dementia. Not to long after using DMSO Daddy had fast mental decline that was Dx as _Early Onset Alzheimer'_s. Not sure if he took it internally as well as rubbing it into his shoulder. I remeber DMSO is to be handled very carefully and never used without gloves and care to not get it in the eyes. I also remember it as being penetrating and taking anything with it would penetrate into the cells.

I have a friend right now who is **taking DMSO internally** for musculoskeletal pain and she told me that she has been having great results using it. I **warned her to be careful** but she says she has read several books on it's use and believes it to be safe. Not sure about strenth's with DMSO. I came today becuase I googled DMSO Jack Kruse and your post came up.

Again I know this is an old thread but I had another friend who had a ganglion cyst three years ago and I shared my experience...
This is what wrote him.

FROM **[Proven Self-Help Remedies To Heal Your Ganglion Cyst](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dopGT5U1f8o) -** **Dr. Alan Mandell, D.C.

Ganglion Cysts- aka Bible Cysts**​

I had one once but I did not know what it was. I was in my early 20's and I was treated by Dr. Roberson DC doctor from the church where John and I met, Dr. Roberson said the cure was to hit my hand hard with a Good sized book.... I can't even remember if I let him hit me or not but that's why it was nicknamed 'Bible Cyst'. Docs hit it with a big book similar to a Bible Size. Mine might have gone away on its own. John and I laughed about this for years! I know we were playing a lot of tennis back then and that was most likely the cause of the Cyst.
**My notes below but watch the video for more of the details.** Dr. Alan Mandell, DC seems like a nice fellow.

-   Can be from repetitive motion
-   Can be from shoes rubbing, Shoes too tight

**To help:**

-   **ARNICA** - at about 5:40 on the video
    [Topical Arnica cream or gel](https://www.vitacost.com/boiron-arnicare-pain-relief-gel) not sure but internal homeopathic Remedy Pills might help too.
    I use arnica for any and all bruising and musculoskeletal pain.

-   **Frankincense Oil** and of **Boswellia**

-   **Tumeric** paste and take Turmeric internally with ginger too all anti-inflammatory,
-   **Massage in and around the cyst**
-   **Warm Compresses Dry heat - _Epsom Salt_ soaks**
-   **Cut out** white processed foods, sugar and high Omega 6 seed oils.
-   **[Quercetin](https://www.vitacost.com/productsearch.aspx?t=Quercetin&mp=1) is good to use**
-   **Tea Tree Oil** mix with coconut oil and put on area
-   **ACV Apple Cider Vinegar on topical and internal I** often add a bottle of Braggs ACV with Mother into bath water.
-   **Walnuts, tart cherries**
-   **Aloe vera** from the fresh leaf leave on 20 min **or wrap and sleep with it on overnight**
-   **Witch Hazel** for Witch Hazel -I recommend [Thayers Witch Hazel Aloe Vera Formula Unscented](https://www.vitacost.com/thayers-witch-hazel-aloe-vera-formula-unscented)

-   **Black Tea bag on cyst---- it will penetrate and help a sty or a cyst.
    (My Note I learned from [EARTH CLINIC](https://www.earthclinic.com/cures/sty.html) ) RE Sty's I've used #4 og **10 Natural Remedies for a Sty (Stye) I was totally amazed that it worked !**
    [https://www.earthclinic.com/cures/sty.html](https://www.earthclinic.com/cures/sty.html)**
-   **Oregano oil**
-   **TAKE AWAY MECHANICAL STRESS, FALL OR REPETITIVE MOTION. MODIFY SHOES.**
-   [Vitacost](https://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-brand-16) **Serrapeptase Enzyme**
    [https://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-serrapeptase-enzyme](https://www.vitacost.com/vitacost-serrapeptase-enzyme)

**Hope this helps anyone dealing with **Ganglion Cysts or a Sty**.**

ps **I take _Maine Coast Sea Vegetables_ Organic Kelp Granules** \-- 1.5 oz **for Iodine**
[https://www.vitacost.com/maine-coast-sea-vegetables-organic-kelp-granules-salt-alternative](https://www.vitacost.com/maine-coast-sea-vegetables-organic-kelp-granules-salt-alternative)

# Thread: New to the group

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5

### REN2BRAINS — 2012-09-17T20:16:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/post-52172>

Hello all,

I have been reading and reading. I haven't started anything yet.

I have Hashimoto's and one Dr. said I probably have fibromyalgia(which I chose to ignore and not take Lyrica etc.)

I have herniated and bulging disks in my neck. I often fight pain and my endo says all my levels are okay but I still feel rotten a lot of the time.

My biggest concern is my daughter and how to get a 19 year old(college freshman and busy busy busy) to start all of this. She is 5ft7in, 240 pounds. When she was five her hormones went crazy and she gained weight like crazy, 50 pounds in 5 months, with no change in diet. Started growing hair in places a 5 year old shouldn't. It finally stopped but she still can't lose weight and just seems to gain. She has had high uric acid, acne, a DNET brain tumor(It was removed in 2008) and now they are saying Crohn's even though her colon is normal. Blood test have high CRP and serum IGE. Her stomach issues started after a campylobacter infection and they gave her 2 or 3 antibiotics. Hopefully I can find some answers here to help her.

### caroline — 2012-09-18T00:37:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/post-52228>

Welcome REN2BRAINS. Keep reading and absorbing and ask questions. You have a lot on your plate and it is so important to help your daughter as fast as possible. Have you looked at the easy start guide? The first thing to do is to start the Leptin RX and then go from there. If you have labs - post them and someone will hack them for you and suggest what else you might need. Dr. Kruse will tell you that you need to fix yourself first and then you can help others - lead by example. Read the journals so you can see how others are working at fixing themselves and maybe start a journal yourself. We are all a work in progress and it is hard work but you will get there.

### samerry50@gmail.com — 2012-09-26T01:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-the-group.3715/post-54288>

I'm from Tennessee and Dr. Kruse just recently fixed a ruptured disc in my back. i'm new to all of this and just embarking on this journey. Have lots to read and learn. Have started on the Paleo diet as I can figure it out so far from the Paleo Answer he asked me to read and the Paleo Diet book. I have a lot of weight to lose but I not only want to lose weight but to improve the muscles in my back so I don't rupture more discs. Also to improve my overall health. I see there is tons of imformation on this site and I'm just beginning to read so I better get started.

# Thread: Jack is ruining my reading enjoyment.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-is-ruining-my-reading-enjoyment.3720/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2012-09-18T00:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-is-ruining-my-reading-enjoyment.3720/post-52226>

Here I was reading this great fiction book about an artist. \[I was all engrossed.\] He was young and dynamic, and he got married and started painting in earnest. \[Sounds good.\] Times were tough and lean, and sometimes they didn't have much for the table. \[Hmm.\] He also spent many nights painting all night long. \[Not good.\] He started becoming successful, but he was developing a few problems. \[Well I could have guessed...\] He finally got some professional help. \[Ohnoes...\] Oh, looks like he had a lithium deficiency. \[Sheesh.\] It was all downhill from there, with continual adjustments in his meds, divorce, institutionalization, etc.

Darn it Jack, is nothing sacred?!? Does anyone know of any Epi-Paleo friendly fiction? Colleen?

### SeaHorse — 2012-09-18T03:32:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-is-ruining-my-reading-enjoyment.3720/post-52264>

Clan of the Cavebear?

(actually I haven't read it, but I think the lighting in the caves would be exemplary)

I'm with you though, I just don't want to read books or hear songs that present the "human condition" as a series of disastrous habits. It's not that I want everything to be "perfect" but I prefer to be inspired rather than depressed by fiction.

### Michael — 2012-09-18T15:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-is-ruining-my-reading-enjoyment.3720/post-52336>

> **Nonchalant;52843 wrote:** Darn it Jack, is nothing sacred?!? Does anyone know of any Epi-Paleo friendly fiction? Colleen?

Let me think on it.

But you might enjoy _Voices of the Old Sea.

[http://www.amazon.com/Voices-Old-Sea-Norman-Lewis/dp/0330345613](http://http://www.amazon.com/Voices-Old-Sea-Norman-Lewis/dp/0330345613)

It's autobiography, but shoud hit some of the right buttons. The author went to Spain to work as a fisherman in the 1950s. So you've got seafood right there

But what's interesting is the fishing village he lived and worked in. It's like something out of the Middle Ages. This is old_ Spain, and by his second season there the area is getting developed for holidaying and it all starts to go. But there's something almost unbeliveably ancient about it up to then. Officially the country is Roman Catholic, but their lives are littered with pre-Christian superstitions and practices. (Maybe you'd be less likely to get this is a protestant country, because it's that further distant?) The women go to church, but the men won't enter the building. The village is full of cats, which they have an odd relationship with - almost as if they venerate them. There's an odd ceremony where every year they row out to an island and beat a girl with ribbons - which feels to me, although the author doesn't say it - like a sublimated relic of a human sacrifice. Every year a travelling medicine man-cum-sorceror visits. And there's still fire-walking in the area. It's like a really ancient form of life, and it's staggering to find it going on only 50 or 60 years ago, and to get some feeling for what it might have been like.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-18T18:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-is-ruining-my-reading-enjoyment.3720/post-52367>

Michael, you have reminded me of "Old Man and the Sea". I remember reading that one aloud to my kids while we were riding in the car. Nice memory.

I think I'll look for your book. And thanks everyone else for the ideas. I need to take a trip to Half Price Books to shop for entertainment that doesn't shoot blue light into my retinas.

### Michael — 2012-09-18T19:54:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-is-ruining-my-reading-enjoyment.3720/post-52431>

> **Nonchalant;52990 wrote:** Michael, you have reminded me of "Old Man and the Sea". I remember reading that one aloud to my kids while we were riding in the car. Nice memory.

Hemingway.

Deceptively simple language - no-one else could really do it, although many tried. He complained once that a visiting journalist had made him sound like a "goddam chickensh\*\*t halfbreed Choctaw Indian". (I guess those were not politically-correct days.) So maybe even his conversation had become spare and simplified. Yet he had a massive collection of books - you can guarantee he'd have been better read than the journalist. And he had made something of a cult of Kipling - a ferociously aesthetic man who would rework and polish sentences until his prose was almost too perfect.

I wonder if you might also enjoy Henry Williamson and Richard Jeffries. There are two able but lesser-known novelists who were also capable naturalists.

There's a Jeffries novel over at Librivox, that imagines civilizational collapse and what might follow, that is read pretty well:

[http://librivox.org/after-london-or-wild-england-by-richard-jefferies/](http://http://librivox.org/after-london-or-wild-england-by-richard-jefferies/)

[http://news.yahoo.com/heart-health-fish-oil-pills-not-answer-study-033643747.html](http://http://news.yahoo.com/heart-health-fish-oil-pills-not-answer-study-033643747.html)

........no surprise to me.

### Joann — 2012-09-18T01:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-studies-showing-no-positive-results.3721/post-52246>

Not enough details in article. At the end it did state people eating fish had benefits rather than supplementing isolated substances which would not have what eating fish would have.

### hellojtm — 2012-09-18T20:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/omega-3-studies-showing-no-positive-results.3721/post-52460>

Yeah Joann they completely contradicted themselves with that final statement since the previous paragraphs say it didn't show any difference.

I eat fish, shellfish, from wild sources whenever I can, within reasonable effort limits. On other days I supplement 1-2g EPA/dha. Some days I do neither. I have a back problem that is very sensitive to dietary induced inflammation, if I have sugar for example 2 scoops of ice cream, it flares up the next day for 1/2 a day. Inclusion of omega3 never helped with that back pain prior to going paleo. However, once I went paleo and thus reduced my omega6 my pain went down an order of magnitude. At that time it was daily for several years.

Sooooo, my point is that I wasn't surprised by this study because I assume those people have excess omega6 that are the underlying cause vs. an n3 deficiency.

But I wonder if that's not why dr. K. wasn't surprised.

### Joann — 2012-09-18T22:53:54+0300

# Thread: Perplexing Thyroid panel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perplexing-thyroid-panel.3722/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-09-18T01:05:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perplexing-thyroid-panel.3722/post-52233>

My previous labs done mid-August, showed a drop in my TSH from .5 to .05. This was the only part of the thyroid panel pulled. So, I ask for a complete panel and get these results today:

TSH/.06 reference .4-4.5

T3f/2.6 reference 2.3-4.2

T4f/1.9 reference .8-1.8

rT3/16

The rT3 was conveyed verbally over the phone, and don't quite know what to make of it.

I was advised to cut my Levothryoxin from 300mcg down to 250mcg. It appears that I am being treated as hyper based on my TSH and T4f, while my T3 looks to be very marginal. JanSz told me last time around to consider dessicated porcine. Sounds reasonable to me based on the polar results of T3 and T4 that I am getting.

Opinions??

### vkiernan — 2012-09-18T01:36:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perplexing-thyroid-panel.3722/post-52239>

I'd do anything but t4 all by itself. Your ft3 is not good and needs to be at the upper range. Got to get the range and u/m for the rt3 and figure out the ratio. With your ft3 where it's at, I'm pretty sure your ratio is not good. Then you need to teach your doc all about this and if he/she isn't willing to listen and help, then it's time to find a new doc. There are plenty of them out there.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-18T01:45:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perplexing-thyroid-panel.3722/post-52243>

Your T4 is at the top of the range.....that is good. Your Free T3 is in the toilet. Your rT3 is coming down.....good. Your doc is only looking at the TSH........it appears to me. If your free T3 is low you need to see the HS CRP, Vitamin D levels, SHBG, TBG, and pregnenolone levels to know truly what is best. If your inflammation is up due to HS CRP, haptoglobin, ferritin or rT3 you should not use TSH as your screening tool for thyroid function to make many clinical decisions because it is very slippery slope. I think you must make an appt and sit down with the doc and be prepared to state your case.

### JanSz — 2012-09-18T03:51:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perplexing-thyroid-panel.3722/post-52267>

> **WannaBeOptimal;52850 wrote:** My previous labs done mid-August, showed a drop in my TSH from .5 to .05. This was the only part of the thyroid panel pulled. So, I ask for a complete panel and get these results today:
>
>
>
> TSH/.06 reference .4-4.5
>
> T3f/2.6 reference 2.3-4.2
>
> T4f/1.9 reference .8-1.8
>
> rT3/16
>
>
>
> The rT3 was conveyed verbally over the phone, and don't quite know what to make of it.
>
>
>
> I was advised to cut my Levothryoxin from 300mcg down to 250mcg. It appears that I am being treated as hyper based on my TSH and T4f, while my T3 looks to be very marginal. JanSz told me last time around to consider dessicated porcine. Sounds reasonable to me based on the polar results of T3 and T4 that I am getting.
>
>
>
> Opinions??

Levothyroxine, also L-thyroxine or T4,

You are eating 300mcg-T4

that is equivalent of 4grains of Natural thyroid

assuming 1(T3)=4(T4)

4grains=(152mcgT4)+(36mcgT3)=304mcgT4

Most people could use 3grains of Natural Thyroid

doses larger than that suppress (excessively) TSH.

That looks like your case.

You may want to switch to Natural Thyroid

I buy mine from Amazon.com (Thyroid-S 1000pills)

If you do, drop all T4

replace it with 2 grains (2 pills) of Thyroid-S

After about 2week to a month you may want try adding another pill.

Aim at TSH(0.5 - 1.5)

As dr Kruse told you, your TSH may not be representative of your status due to possible variety situations.

Consider making more detailed checkup.

///

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-18T03:53:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perplexing-thyroid-panel.3722/post-52269>

I hate synthetic T4 drugs......just saying.

>
>

>

This thing is coming with Obama care.

I know this system, I lived in communist Poland.

Only Communist Party members and some labor union had access to prosthesis for hip replacement.

When my Mom need that prosthesis in 1975, I had to get it from England, go to Poland, and read doc instructions what to do with it. Then kick my Mom out of the bed on the third day after surgery (to the horror of that doc.

My Mom was his first patient to actually walk.

There is 48 days to elections.

There is your chance. Do not complain afterward.

# Thread: Young men obesity sheds more light on BG 12:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-18T02:42:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-52253>

[http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/cen.12018/abstract](http://http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/cen.12018/abstract)

This shows that serum testosterone alone is destroyed when brain signaling is altered by brain inflammation. Obesity is an inflammatory brain condition and low T is real concern. It is not a SHBG or E2 alone story. It is an inflammation LR story.

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-09-21T09:02:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-53135>

Fascinating. Back when I weighed 265 lbs and had clinically low Free T, I was told it was due to high SHBG produced by all the excess fat. Went paleo soon thereafter and over the next 2.5 years lost over 125 lbs....but just shrank, not changing my feminine curvy shape (moobs and hips). Mind you, all the big names in Paleo-land said that eating paleo vs low-fat/high carb would cause you to change body shape and improve body comp as you lost weight. Not true for me, even though I did everything right by Paleo standards. I went from being a bearded obese moob-monster to a mushy skinny-fat little girl with a hairy rack.

Since starting Epi-Paleo and respecting the light cycles by aggressively wearing blue blockers after sunset and making sure all electronic displays are no higher color temp than midday sun (5500K) during the daytime, I have now gone a total of 139 lbs down and my body is started to get a more vaguely masculine shape to it. Moobs have shrunk more in the past month than they have in the past 3 years and my waist-to-hip ratio is looking like it's starting to equalize.

Can't wait to get back into CT hardcore and see how that furthers things. I might finally actually get a man-like body for the first time in my life, at 31 years old.

Thank you so much more than I can ever possibly express, Dr. Kruse. I'm tearing up as I type this. Words fail.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-21T17:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-53183>

### nonchalant — 2012-09-21T17:52:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-53197>

> **CuiviÃ«;53770 wrote:**
>
> Can't wait to get back into CT hardcore and see how that furthers things.

I'd say don't wait. I started the ice baths again a month ago and I'm glad I did. Just let the bath water sit overnight and it gets down to room temp. Then a reasonable amount of ice gets it just right. You will find that you can quickly adjust to CT again.

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-09-21T19:27:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-53220>

> **Nonchalant;53832 wrote:** I'd say don't wait. I started the ice baths again a month ago and I'm glad I did. Just let the bath water sit overnight and it gets down to room temp. Then a reasonable amount of ice gets it just right. You will find that you can quickly adjust to CT again.

Oh, I'm already doing ice baths--just not able to them 'hardcore' yet. I'm just only able to keep skin temps between 50-55 for 20 min before the water warms up too much. I'm already maxed out on ice capacity (taking two days to freeze 30 1-quart containers for ~60 lbs of ice, plus three ice packs from the Cool Fat Burner), and it takes that much to cool down 78 degree SoCal tap water in a stuffy 90+ degree apartment.

Thanks for the suggestions. I tried leaving a tub full overnight, but it only cooled the 78 degree water down to 76, and I have a roommate who needs to shower in the morning.

Given that I'm already at peak ice capacity, I'll just have to make do with 20 minute sessions and spot-CT until the tap cools down and the extra ice can be used to lengthen the sessions up to the recommended 45 minutes and/or lower the temp down more (I find I have to get skin temp down to 48 before I get the cherry-red/lobster effect, so my body might need a lower temp range in general; when I did CT this past winter I had to get the water down to 46 for the lobster-red/skin-on-fire/internal furnace ignition to happen, anything warmer and I just get pale white and severely fatigued).

Incidentally, didn't see much moob reduction back then; it seems to be the Epi-Paleo and serious minding of light cycles (as much as I can on an evening shift job) and control of exposure to artifcial blue light that induced the most substantial change. Now that those elements are in place I wonder if the CT in the new context will have an interaction effect.

Rest assured, Project Moob-Be-Gone is well underway, just waiting until conditions permit it to be cranked up to the next level.

### hope2conceive — 2012-10-14T04:39:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-58033>

Since starting Epi-Paleo and respecting the light cycles by aggressively wearing blue blockers after sunset and making sure all electronic displays are no higher color temp than midday sun (5500K) during the daytime

Cuivie,

how do you change your color temp on say smartphone?

### cuivie@me.com — 2012-10-17T05:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/young-men-obesity-sheds-more-light-on-bg-12.3724/post-58667>

> **hope2conceive;59371 wrote:** Cuivie,
>
>
>
> how do you change your color temp on say smartphone?

I jailbroke my iPhone and iPad and installed f.lux on them. And after sunset I always wear blue blocker glasses regardless.

If you have a smartphone that f.lux isn't ported to, or if our have the latest version of the iPhone or iOS (for which there isn't a jailbreak), then take a look at Gunnar glasses. They don't block all blue, but do block excess blue. I'm thinking about getting some in my prescription. That would take car of any device and all other artificial light during the daytime.

In college I can remember instinctively ducking when someone would say it. Now it is a common word with kids, mine included, but it still bothers me.

### ealachan — 2012-09-18T16:49:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-farts.3725/post-52343>

> **MartiD;52955 wrote:** I grew up in a household where you could get away with saying almost any four letter word except fart. My dad would swing when he heard it, word or action. In college I can remember instinctively ducking when someone would say it. Now it is a common word with kids, mine included, but it still bothers me.

Your dad and my dad may well be polar opposites. My dad texts me when he's cut a particularly horrid fart and grosses himself out...LOL

# Thread: Question about HRT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-hrt.3727/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sem — 2012-09-18T04:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-hrt.3727/post-52276>

Hi everyone - my doctor put me on HRT (.75 vivelle dot and 100mg micronized progesterone). I've been on it for about a month. My doctor told me I was in early menopause and would not get my period even on the HRT. Is it good that I got it? Let me tell you, it has been brutal. I was so sick the night before and yesterday and couldn't figure out why until my old friend showed up!

### Sem — 2012-09-19T22:58:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-hrt.3727/post-52765>

Bumping to see if anyone has any ideas. Just confused about whether I should consider it a good or bad thing. I emailed my doctor and she seemed confused too!

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-19T23:06:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-hrt.3727/post-52768>

you might be peri-menopausal w/annovulatory cycles.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-20T01:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-hrt.3727/post-52807>

I got my period after six months of nothing and now have had them for four months. Your hormones were probably so out of whack that your body may not have known what to do. Now it says ahhhhhhh we finally have something to work with and gave you the present of TOM (or Aunt Flo as South Park refers to it).

# Thread: Recent blog comment
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-blog-comment.3728/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-09-18T11:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-blog-comment.3728/post-52311>

This post by Jack in the comments to BG12 interested me. (I can't post there, because it's a different log-in, and I've screwed up my password somehow.)

> ... Many of the gym onwers and Phdâ€™s in paleo have this belief that exercise is critical. What other species lift weights in a gym or does WOD? I think Erwan LeCorre is so far ahead of the rest of the paleo krewe on this topic it is not funnyâ€¦â€¦.he moves naturally like a human should.

That clicked immediately. As soon as I heard what Erwan was doing it interested me. I heard of him through reading some stuff by "Barefoot Ted" having got interested in minimalist footwear and so on. In those days I don't think the paleo people were even aware of him.

I can remember first reading about Erwan's concept of the "zoo human" and my immediate thought was that that was a bit rude. But the more you think about it the more you see it makes a lot of sense. We're just in this state where what should be natural and what we're ideally fitted for by descent is alien to us, and what is unnatural and even harmful to us seems normal. I think we just don't see the abnormality (in biological terms) of what's our usual environment. That's not just movement, which Erwan specializes in. It's not even that plus food. It's so many things. There's sleep. There's blue light. There's the cold: as I guess Jack would point out, we put a jumper on if it gets a little cold, but few hunter-gatherers would bother to add more clothing unless it got _really_ bitter. We sit in chairs for hours. We go by car instead of walking. We allow chemical companies to panic us about "cleanliness" and treat our homes and persons with harmful products. We wear sunblock. And after twenty or thirty years of this if we were put back in our natural environment, that evolution fitted us for, we could no longer cope with it. We are members of a human zoo.

And its kind of symptomatic that when we recognize that our physical capabilities aren't what they should be our notion is that we go indoors somewhere and perform repetitive and highly stylized motions in a kind of industrialized conception of how to "get fit".

Yeah, I think Erwan is light years ahead.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-18T13:57:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-blog-comment.3728/post-52316>

There is no doubt he is.

### Dali Dula — 2012-09-18T13:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-blog-comment.3728/post-52317>

Not light years ahead, 10,000 or so years behind. Dial it all right-food, sleep, light, temp,activity and everything clicks. This is the path to optimal. And it feels good. Thanks, Jack.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-18T17:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-blog-comment.3728/post-52359>

Having an aversion to oysters, or shellfish, or seafood in general is ironic.

### JoeBranca — 2012-09-21T07:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recent-blog-comment.3728/post-53127>

I took a Movnat course a couple of months back and can't recommend it enough for anyone who is not confident in natural adaptive movements. It's hard not to gush about it, the pragmatic skills we need to re-learn, but also the mindset in which it is delivered. I feel personally implicated because I've known many of these things throughout life, they're intuitive, but turned my back willingly for more of a life of convenience and the comforts of technology. As if the immaterial self is all that mattered. The "wild" is mediated through packaged and contained experiences designated as escapes from the main course of life. Compartmentalizing or ignoring the wild, if not paving it over with a parking lot, how has that worked out for us?

We are already highly adapted to these wild natural environments we find ourselves in, to move exist and breathe in. It is therefore way more bizarre to NOT do something like MovNat. The fact that it even has to be a movement or organization needing to grow in popularity, or worse written off as another trendy cutting edge idea for paleo hipsters, is pathetic in the big scheme. I mean think about it, how to walk, how to breathe, how to move, has to be demonstrated and taught in the realm of commerce, which doesn't speak so much of the exploits of the teachers as the profound rarity of the jewel of how to physically exist as the most adaptive creatures on the planet that we already are. So onward with turning around this WALL-E esque disaster.

# Thread: High Albumin Globulin Ratio
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-albumin-globulin-ratio.3729/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Destiny — 2012-09-18T13:16:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-albumin-globulin-ratio.3729/post-52314>

High Albumin Globulin Ratio - is this something to worry about?

**Albumin **4.9 3.6 -5.1 g/dl

**Globulin Calculated **2.1 2.2 - 3.9 g/dl Low

**A/G ratio** 2.3** 1.0 - 2.1 g/l High

I found this:

A high A/G ratio is used to indicate under production of immunoglobulins**. This condition is seen due to presence of some genetic disorders, hypothyroidism**, high protein/high carbohydrate diet**, Glucocorticoid excess, low levels of globulin (hypogammaglobulinemia) and leukemia.

I have been taking T3 for over a month. So maybe this should fix it?

# Thread: The Power of Good Food
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lioness7 — 2012-09-18T18:04:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52363>

Hi everyone,

I don't post much, but today I felt compelled to share a link and keep this as brief as possible not to bore you with the details.

September is Childhood Cancer Awareness Month. I get a bit reflective and emotional in September as I am the mom of a childhood cancer survivor. Five years ago, my 4yr old son was diagnosed with leukemia. This still happened to him despite exclusive breast feeding, no vaccinations and as pure, clean and organic of a diet I could provide. There are no guarantees in life, so the "crunchy" mom learned. He was on chemo/steroids/etc. for exactly 1,086 days (yes , I counted every day, each day, with a positive outlook for the moment he would finish). I fully and truly believe that further tweaking his diet during this period prevented all of the regular side effects of chemo - he had none, and it allowed him to tolerate the treatment to even the marvel of his oncs. I also wish that Dr. Kruse's website was around during his treatment, as I would have become even more knowledgeable than I actually believed I was at the time. I am trying to implement as much **Epi** Paleo as I possibly can on my family, especially him.

I have referred this site as well as others to many parents, but I highly doubt that any have visited and if they did they most likely never came back.....so sad and unfortunate. I was always the bit of a sh\*t disturber on the kids cancer forums, presenting opposing info/views related to diet, etc, in as polite a way as possible. Eventually I gave up....I had a "you can lead a horse to water"..... moment and stopped trying to change the world as I found it draining when I would read about the horrific side effects these children suffered which could have potentially been reduced, fixed, or avoided had it not been for parental reluctance and onc ignorance.

Below is the link that inspired my ill written post this morning. Their story was brought to my attention a few years back.

[http://www.180degreefarm.org/Mason\_s\_Story.php](http://http://www.180degreefarm.org/Mason_s_Story.php)

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-18T18:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52372>

Thanks for this post - inspiring, and makes me count my blessings.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-18T19:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52396>

Hi Lioness, great to hear from you. You and your son's story is awesome. No side effects?? That is truly amazing.

Your son is so blessed to have you protecting and watching out for him.

I hope you don't mind, but I've stopped giving to cancer societies, as well as heart and diabetes, MS, etc etc.

They make me so angry. I feel they do not have the patients' best interests in mind.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-18T19:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52400>

> **Nonchalant;53019 wrote:** Hi Lioness, great to hear from you. You and your son's story is awesome. No side effects?? That is truly amazing.
>
> Your son is so blessed to have you protecting and watching out for him.
>
>
>
> I hope you don't mind, but I've stopped giving to cancer societies, as well as heart and diabetes, MS, etc etc.
>
> They make me so angry. I feel they do not have the patients' best interests in mind.

This as been a peeve of mine for a while as well. . .any group that has a bracelet I want no part of. WHAT ABOUT BUYING A PLASTIC BRACELET HELPS PEOPLE?!?!? For gods sake, find a neighbor in need and help them. If we all did that. . .

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-18T19:34:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52413>

> **Nonchalant;53019 wrote:** I hope you don't mind, but I've stopped giving to cancer societies, as well as heart and diabetes, MS, etc etc.
>
> They make me so angry. I feel they do not have the patients' best interests in mind.

Agree! I have a friend who runs a small cancer society and everyone is a volunteer and 100% the profits go to an organization that does research. She is very picky and doesn't donate money. She has the research facilities send proposals for what they want to her for review. She then takes the donated money and buys the microscopes and other equipment for the research facility. At least the money is not going to pay for someone's salary although it's probably not helping much anyway.

Maybe this forum's revolution, lead by Jack, will help change the minds of cancer patients to look elsewhere to help!

### caroline — 2012-09-19T01:59:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52566>

Thanks for posting your story. You are a remarkable woman. I have always tried to help a neighbour/friend when they are in need as opposed to giving money. You sound like a starfish to me!

### Destiny — 2012-09-19T02:46:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52573>

Thanks for sharing... it is very powerful.

### kjgraffin@yahoo.com — 2012-09-19T14:52:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52663>

I didn't realize this about September. It hits home today as I have a coworker who is going to lose her 11yo daughter any day now to leukemia....

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-19T17:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52691>

THanks for sharing your family's story.

### lioness7 — 2012-09-19T19:54:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52734>

Thank you all for your kind words. I never want to speak of this experience to make people feel bad or sorry, but to turn it into a positive one. When you are handed lemons, you try to make the greatest lemonade you can.....so I washed the lemons, made sure I had the best water I could get, used the healthiest sweetner I could find and put the whole thing in the nicest pitcher I had and was darned thrilled to have lots of ice on hand.

I can completely feel and imagine the frustrations that Dr. K feels having to explain/defend/reference everthing to not just patients, family and friends, but his collegues as well. I understand the stress and anxiety of forum members trying to convince their PCP's of specific tests and the raised eyebrows when one tries to dispel popularly held beliefs/medical urban legends. My wish is that everyone were as open minded as the members here are....and of course that all doctors were trained to be dot connecting instead of simply medicating.

Thankfully, during the first month of txt, I was able to gather up myself, use some nutritional background and intuition for guidance to help not only my son but also also for the sake of everyone in his life witnessing this battle. The fruits of my labour simply drove me further each day. I laugh today thinking about all the late night hours at the hospital and at home that I spent on line in search of what ?????????, causes, options, studies, stories of hope, relapse rates, ???? only to mess myself all up circadian wise...lol.

I could not bear to watch the orange chemo IV go into him during those ten days that we were in patient, so much so, that I had to leave the room and cry in the hallway during the infusions as the drano was poured into his veins. This perfect image of health I thought in my mind, who in his four years had not been to a dr. since he was delivered - what went amiss? The nurses must have laughed for sure when I left the room, for so early into the treatment, little did i know that I would spend the next three years putting chemo into him every single night myself. I feared the drugs more than the disease. But my true moment of strength came when the bone marrow aspirate hit a nerve causing him writhing shooting pains up his spine, worse than any woman in labour I had every seen. I actually had a flash in my mind of picking him up and jumping out the window.........I stopped and said to myself this is day 3 and we have 1083 more days to go?.........no f\*\*\*n way!!!!!!!!!! After discharge, I drew an imaginary line: us and them. They did their thing and I did mine. In my mind, They were only chemo/steroid/Septra experts (who still made a few mistakes) I had complete control over everything else and of course what went into him.... a very "they don't ask / I won't tell relationship was about to unfold! I perfected the polite nod and smile. To avoid the ravages of txt, to not have more than necessary hospital stays (I had 7 other children and my husband, the only income earner, to also consider during all this) to give my son the quality of life that a four year old was supposed to have, and of course to increase his chances of eventfree survival - I vowed to myself that I would do everything that I could even if it killed me

Despite all the gold mine of info that I have read here, there is one thing that resonates with me the most and what I have always believed: “Think of food as hormone information, not as a metabolic fuel" - I wish everyone looked at it that way.

Now, if I could only wear the blueblockers less, by going to bed earlier..........next project -> fix mom.........

### Stavity — 2012-09-19T23:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52770>

I agree with Caroline....you are a STARFISH!!!

Please keep posting and sharing how you are fixing you, mom.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-19T23:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-power-of-good-food.3735/post-52781>

This perfect image of health I thought in my mind, who in his four years had not been to a dr. since he was delivered - what went amiss? The nurses must have laughed for sure when I left the room, for so early into the treatment, little did i know that I would spend the next three years putting chemo into him every single night myself. I feared the drugs more than the disease. But my true moment of strength came when the bone marrow aspirate hit a nerve causing him writhing shooting pains up his spine, worse than any woman in labour I had every seen. I actually had a flash in my mind of picking him up and jumping out the window.........I stopped and said to myself this is day 3 and we have 1083 more days to go?.........no f\*\*\*n way!!!!!!!!!! After discharge, I drew an imaginary line: us and them. They did their thing and I did mine. In my mind, They were only chemo/steroid/Septra experts (who still made a few mistakes) I had complete control over everything else and of course what went into him.... a very "they don't ask / I won't tell relationship was about to unfold! I perfected the polite nod and smile. To avoid the ravages of txt, to not have more than necessary hospital stays (I had 7 other children and my husband, the only income earner, to also consider during all this) to give my son the quality of life that a four year old was supposed to have, and of course to increase his chances of eventfree survival - I vowed to myself that I would do everything that I could even if it killed me

### Meg — 2012-09-20T00:13:13+0300
